descriptionHtml:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Head of Product (B2B SaaS) in India.

This role is a high-impact leadership position focused on shaping and scaling a product suite powering influencer-led growth and performance marketing infrastructure. You will own end-to-end product strategy, execution, and delivery across multiple product lines, with a strong initial focus on building and scaling a core flagship product. The environment is fast-paced, highly iterative, and deeply customer-driven, requiring strong product instincts and the ability to operate in ambiguity. You will work closely with engineering, design, and leadership teams to translate customer insights into scalable product solutions. The role blends strategic thinking with hands-on execution, from roadmap ownership to sprint planning and product delivery. You will also play a key role in defining product metrics, improving user experience, and ensuring consistent shipping velocity. This is a leadership opportunity to build category-defining SaaS infrastructure in a rapidly evolving market.

Accountabilities

  • Own the end-to-end product vision, roadmap, and execution across multiple B2B SaaS product lines, ensuring alignment with company strategy and customer needs.
  • Lead customer discovery efforts, including interviews, user research, beta testing, and ongoing feedback loops to deeply understand user pain points and churn drivers.
  • Drive product prioritisation, trade-offs, and sprint planning in close collaboration with engineering and design teams.
  • Own the development and scaling of a core product (initial focus area), ensuring strong execution from concept to delivery.
  • Define product requirements, write detailed specs, and ensure high-quality execution across design, engineering, and UX.
  • Build, track, and optimise key product metrics such as activation, retention, engagement, and usage.
  • Monitor market trends, competitive landscape, and ecosystem shifts (DTC, Shopify, affiliate marketing, influencer growth) to inform product strategy.
  • Ensure high standards of user experience, design consistency, and product usability across all releases.
  • Lead and mentor product and design teams, fostering a culture of ownership, speed, and quality.
  • Collaborate closely with leadership and cross-functional teams to align product direction with business objectives.

Requirements

  • 8+ years of product management experience, ideally in B2B SaaS environments.
  • Proven experience building products from 0→1, with strong execution and scaling capabilities.
  • Strong product judgment with the ability to make clear prioritisation and trade-off decisions.
  • Deep customer-first mindset with strong user research and discovery skills.
  • Strong design sensibility with attention to UX, usability, and product quality.
  • Experience working in fast-paced, high-ambiguity environments requiring ownership and autonomy.
  • Familiarity with DTC, Shopify, influencer marketing, or affiliate ecosystems is highly desirable.
  • Strong analytical mindset with experience defining and tracking product metrics.
  • Excellent communication and leadership skills, with the ability to align stakeholders across engineering and business teams.
  • Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution and delivery focus.

Benefits

  • Competitive salary: ₹3,000,000 - ₹4,000,000 per year
  • Fully remote working model
  • High-impact leadership role with ownership of core product strategy
  • Opportunity to build category-defining B2B SaaS infrastructure
  • Fast-paced, high-ownership startup environment
  • Direct collaboration with founders and senior leadership
  • Strong autonomy in product decision-making
  • Exposure to cutting-edge influencer and performance marketing ecosystems
  • Opportunity to lead and grow product and design teams

How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

descriptionHtml:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title And Summary

Director – AI Strategy & Product Management (Agentic AI)

________________________________________

Director – AI Strategy & Product Management (Agentic AI)

Operational Intelligence | Mastercard Services

Location: Pune / Gurgaon | Level: Director (L5)

________________________________________

Overview

Mastercard’s Operational Intelligence (OI) team is building agentic AI and LLM-driven platforms to transform payment operations.

We are hiring a Director – AI Strategy & Product Management to lead strategy, product development, UX, and execution of enterprise AI products, including multi-agent systems, decision platforms, and autonomous workflows.

This is a hands-on 0→1 leadership role evolving into team and program leadership at scale.

________________________________________

Key Responsibilities

AI Strategy & Product Leadership

  • Define agentic AI strategy and roadmap across OI
  • Own 0→1→scale lifecycle (PRD → MVP → Production)
  • Deliver AI products across reconciliation, disputes, and operations


________________________________________

Agentic Systems & Architecture Alignment

  • Define product requirements for:
  • Multi-agent systems (LangGraph, LangChain, etc.)
  • Stateful workflows, guardrails, and HITL
  • Partner with engineering on LLMs, RAG, Graph RAG, and MCP-based systems


________________________________________

Agentic UI/UX Design

  • Design beyond chat UX:
  • Structured workflows, dashboards, and controls
  • Build:
  • Trust layer (explainability, transparency)
  • Control layer (HITL, overrides)
  • Observability layer (agent actions, traces)


________________________________________

Program Office & Portfolio Management

  • Establish and lead AI Program Office (PMO)
  • Manage portfolio of AI products, platforms, and initiatives
  • Drive prioritization, capacity planning, and executive reporting


________________________________________

POC & Experimentation Leadership

  • Lead POC → MVP → Production pipeline
  • Define success metrics (business + technical)
  • Ensure scalable design and avoid “POC traps”


________________________________________

Data, Models & Platform Integration

  • Drive integration of:
  • LLMs (GPT, Claude, LLaMA, etc.)
  • Enterprise data platforms
  • Define requirements for:
  • Retrieval (vector + graph)
  • Memory systems (short/long-term, semantic, graph)


________________________________________

Observability, Governance & Evaluation

  • Define frameworks for:
  • Agent observability (AgentOps)
  • Evaluation (accuracy, grounding, latency)
  • Responsible AI and compliance


________________________________________

Business Impact & GTM

  • Partner with engineering and commercial teams
  • Drive product adoption, monetization, and ROI
  • Track automation, cost savings, and revenue uplift


________________________________________

Qualifications

  • 12–15+ years in AI/ML product management or technical strategy
  • Proven experience building AI/GenAI products (0→1→scale)
  • Strong understanding of:
  • LLMs, RAG, Graph RAG, agentic systems
  • Experience working closely with engineering on scalable platforms


Preferred: Payments, fintech, or operational intelligence experience

Corporate Security Responsibility

 All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Vice President Portfolio Operations

You’ll be the senior-most representative on-site, responsible for team growth, operational excellence, and culture.Key ResponsibilitiesOwn day-to-day operations and serve as the primary point of contact for the Data & Integration Operations team.Lead and mentor our cross-functional team across data analytics, support, and operations.Drive hiring, onboarding, and ongoing development of local talent.Build a strong, values-aligned culture rooted in trust, autonomy, and excellence.Collaborate closely with leadership across product, engineering, and finance to ensure strategic alignment.Oversee performance and reporting for the local data analytics team, ensuring insights inform business decisions.Contribute to site-level planning, operational processes, and delivery tracking.Requirements10+ years of people and team management experience.Prior experience leading a team of 25+ people.Strong background in financial services, fintech, or related operational roles.Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams in high-growth environments.Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.Strategic thinker with a hands-on, problem-solving mindset.Must be based full-time in Coimbatore, with occasional travel to other Arcana locations.Nice to HaveExperience working with or managing distributed/global teams.Comfort navigating the fast pace and ambiguity of startup environments.
descriptionHtml:
We’re hiring a dynamic and experienced Leader to head our Data & Integration Operations team. This role requires a strong mix of leadership, execution, and collaboration, ideally with experience in fintech or financial services. You’ll be the senior-most representative on-site, responsible for team growth, operational excellence, and culture.



Key Responsibilities

  • Own day-to-day operations and serve as the primary point of contact for the Data & Integration Operations team.
  • Lead and mentor our cross-functional team across data analytics, support, and operations.
  • Drive hiring, onboarding, and ongoing development of local talent.
  • Build a strong, values-aligned culture rooted in trust, autonomy, and excellence.
  • Collaborate closely with leadership across product, engineering, and finance to ensure strategic alignment.
  • Oversee performance and reporting for the local data analytics team, ensuring insights inform business decisions.
  • Contribute to site-level planning, operational processes, and delivery tracking.



Requirements

  • 10+ years of people and team management experience.
  • Prior experience leading a team of 25+ people.
  • Strong background in financial services, fintech, or related operational roles.
  • Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams in high-growth environments.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Strategic thinker with a hands-on, problem-solving mindset.
  • Must be based full-time in Coimbatore, with occasional travel to other Arcana locations.



Nice to Have

  • Experience working with or managing distributed/global teams.
  • Comfort navigating the fast pace and ambiguity of startup environments.

title: Sr. Director, Product Management (Core Products)
description: About UsHighLevel is an AI-powered business operating system that gives agencies, entrepreneurs and SMBs the infrastructure to build, automate and scale. Today, HighLevel supports SMBs across 150+ countries, fueling community-driven growth rooted in real customer outcomes.To date, businesses operating on HighLevel have generated over $7 billion in ecosystem value, demonstrating the impact of shared infrastructure at scale. By centralizing conversations, automation and intelligence into one system, we help businesses move faster, reduce complexity and execute efficiently.Behind the platform, HighLevel powers more than 4 billion API hits and 2.5 billion message events daily. descriptionHtml:
About Us

HighLevel is an AI-powered business operating system that gives agencies, entrepreneurs and SMBs the infrastructure to build, automate and scale. Today, HighLevel supports SMBs across 150+ countries, fueling community-driven growth rooted in real customer outcomes.

To date, businesses operating on HighLevel have generated over $7 billion in ecosystem value, demonstrating the impact of shared infrastructure at scale. By centralizing conversations, automation and intelligence into one system, we help businesses move faster, reduce complexity and execute efficiently.

Behind the platform, HighLevel powers more than 4 billion API hits and 2.5 billion message events daily. With 250 terabytes of distributed data, 250+ microservices and over 1 million domain names supported, our architecture is built for performance, resilience and long-term scalability.

Our people

With over 2,000 team members across 10+ countries, HighLevel operates as a global, remote-first organization built for speed and ownership. We value initiative, clarity and execution, creating space for ambitious people to build systems that support millions of businesses worldwide. Here, innovation thrives, ideas are celebrated and people come first, no matter where they call home.

Our impact

Every month, HighLevel enables more than 1.5 billion messages, 200 million leads and 20 million conversations for the more than 1 million businesses we support. Behind those numbers are real people building independence, expanding opportunity and creating measurable impact. We’re proud to be a part of that.

Learn more about us on our YouTube Channel or Blog Posts

Why This Role Exists

The Sr. Director, Product Management (Platform & Core Products) will lead the foundational layer of HighLevel's product stack, spanning infrastructure platforms, automation capabilities, CRM primitives, and shared systems like identity, permissions, notifications, and audit logging.

This is a critical org-wide leadership role, owning HighLevel’s platform extensibility, data model reliability, cross-surface consistency, and internal product development velocity. You will manage a team of 25–30 PMs via 3 Group Product Managers and drive strategy, execution, and org design for a portfolio that underpins everything from marketing automation and conversation management to AI-driven workflows and CRM object modeling.

You’ll be expected to scale a high-performance product org, influence product direction across pods, and partner closely with engineering to deliver a scalable, secure, and elegant platform for millions of users.

Strategic Priorities

  • Build an Extensible Platform: Architect reusable primitives (identity, roles, objects, notifications, reporting) that enable faster development across HighLevel.
  • Modernize Core CRM: Scale performance and usability across contacts, companies, tasks, opportunities, and conversations — the backbone of daily SMB workflows.
  • Unlock Scalable Automation: Advance our workflows engine and Workflow AI to power increasingly complex use cases with speed and simplicity.
  • Drive UX Consistency Across Surfaces: Ensure UI, interaction patterns, and product language are coherent across contact, sub-account, and agency layers.
  • Support Data-Driven Product Maturity: Enforce strong instrumentation, analytics readiness, and reporting across all core products.
  • Operationalize Product Management Excellence: Raise the bar for product planning, prioritization, execution, and accountability across 30+ PMs and APMs.

Key Responsibilities

  • Define and drive the long term product vision, roadmap, and success metrics for platform and core product areas.
  • Manage and coach a team ofGroup PMs and PMs,.cultivating a strong leadership bench and ensuring high performance.
  • Serve as the bridge between platform engineering, infra, and product — enabling other teams to move faster through well-designed interfaces and reusable systems.
  • Set quality standards for API design, system performance, scalability, and reliability.
  • Lead cross-functional coordination with design, engineering, QA, support, data, and legal to deliver on-time, high-quality launches.
  • Represent the needs of agencies, sub-accounts, and end contacts in platform and CRM decisions — ensuring we build with deep user empathy.
  • Collaborate with the Product leadership to influence org-wide product strategy, culture, and planning cadences.
  • Lead strategic quarterly and annual planning for the org — bringing clarity to priorities across technical debt, product innovation, and user experience.
  • Act as a thought partner to engineering leadership in shaping architecture and development investment decisions.

What Success Looks Like

  • HighLevel’s platform teams are recognised as enablers, not blockers — other pods build faster using your team's APIs, patterns, and systems.
  • CRM primitives and workflows become more reliable, performant, and deeply adopted by our users — visible in activation and retention metrics.
  • The organization experiences clean product ownership, predictable roadmaps, and fast decision-making under your leadership.
  • Workflow AI and Workflow Core support a growing number of use cases without sacrificing speed, reliability, or clarity.
  • The data model becomes flexible and extensible, enabling custom objects, relationships, and analytics without friction.
  • You are viewed as a strategic thought partners and executional anchor for platform and core products at HighLevel

Ideal Candidate Profile

  • 12–15+ years in product management, with at least 5+ years managing managers (GPMs,Directors or equivalents).
  • Experience scaling platform and core product orgs in fast-growth B2B SaaS companies, (ideally between $500M-$1B ARR).
  • Deep fluency in CRM, automation platforms, or workflow engines — understands data models, APIs, and system-level product thinking.
  • Prior experience building internal platforms or developer tools that support multiple product teams.
  • Ability to scale orgs, implement processes, and hire/coach high-performing PMs.
  • Technical fluency — capable of earning trust with engineering leads and making calls on infra trade-offs, architecture, and scalability.
  • Familiar with two-tier SaaS, multi-tenant environments, and role-based access control models.
  • Strong written and asynchronous communication skills — capable of driving clarity across functions and time zones.

Bonus Points For

  • Experience working on workflow builders, CRM platforms, or marketing automation tools.
  • Background in B2B2SMB or agency-led SaaS models.
  • Exposure to design systems, shared component libraries, or analytics platforms.
  • Hands-on familiarity with platform performance metrics (e.g., P95 latency, Sentry, Grafana).
  • Experience integrating with third-party platforms like Stripe, Meta, Google, or TikTok (even if not leading monetization).

Equal Employment Opportunity Information

The company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. As an employer subject to affirmative action regulations, we invite you to voluntarily provide the following demographic information. This information is used solely for compliance with government recordkeeping, reporting, and other legal requirements. Providing this information is voluntary and refusal to do so will not affect your application status. This data will be kept separate from your application and will not be used in the hiring decision.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

descriptionHtml:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title And Summary

Director, Product Management, Advanced Analytics & AI

Director, Product Management, Advanced Analytics & AI

Mastercard’s Services organization is a key growth engine, delivering data-driven insights, analytics, and AI-powered solutions that enable clients to make smarter decisions and achieve better outcomes. Within Services, the Business & Market Insights (BMI) organization builds and commercialises analytics, experimentation, and decisioning products across financial and non-financial industries.

Overview

The Advanced Analytics & AI team applies sophisticated analytics, AI, and Mastercard data assets to solve complex industry problems at scale, working across product, sales, and delivery teams in a matrixed global environment.

The Services team is a key differentiator for Mastercard, providing the cutting-edge services that help our customers make smarter decisions and drive better outcomes. Focused on thinking big and scaling fast around the globe, this agile team is responsible for end-to-end solutions for a diverse global customer base. Centred on data-driven technologies and innovation, these services include consulting & innovation services in payments and beyond, data insights and analytics solutions as well as loyalty and marketing programs and platforms.

Within the Services organization, the BMI product organization is focused on creating and delivering superior products for our clients in the area of operational & location insights, business intelligence, analytics and data driven experimentation. This team is accountable for ensuring we are growing our business through thoughtful product road mapping that evolves our solution sets to meet existing and new use cases, influencing global product managers to ensure the solutions in region are effective and partnering with our Business Development and Client Services teams to drive relevant go-to-market solutions for our customers in the region.

Title and Summary

Director, Product Management, Advanced Analytics & AI

Role

This role is responsible for industry-level product management for the Advanced Analytics product line for the Asia Pacific region. This role is responsible for industry-level P&L ownership and growth for Advanced Analytics & AI solutions within the relevant industry portfolio assigned across the Asia Pacific region.

The Director serves as the commercial and strategic owner of the industry portfolio, accountable for revenue growth, client adoption, and value realisation across our suite of multiple data, analytics and AI products. The role partners closely with product-led P&L owners, regional business development and delivery teams, and global product management teams to maximise total portfolio performance.

The role reports to the Vice President, Product Management, Advanced Analytics (Asia Pacific).

Key Responsibilities

Industry P&L Ownership & Growth

  • Own revenue, growth targets, and commercial performance for Advanced Analytics within the relevant industries assigned across APAC
  • Shape industry-specific growth strategies leveraging the full Advanced Analytics & AI product portfolio
  • Balance short-term commercial outcomes with long-term scalability and margin discipline


Client & Market Leadership

  • Act as the senior industry lead for Advanced Analytics & AI, engaging senior executive-level stakeholders
  • Drive solution positioning, value articulation, and deal shaping for complex client opportunities
  • Identify unmet industry needs and translate them into scalable analytics opportunities


Portfolio Commercialisation

  • Partner with product-led P&L owners to adapt and commercialise analytics products for industry-specific use cases
  • Prioritise product adoption based on industry economics, client readiness, and revenue potential
  • Ensure disciplined use of customisation versus scalable solutions


Go-To-Market & Execution

  • Work closely with Business Development, Client Services, and Delivery teams to execute industry GTM plans
  • Support pipeline development, large deals, and strategic partnerships
  • Oversee delivery quality and client outcomes within the industry portfolio


Strategic Input & Thought Leadership

  • Provide structured market feedback to influence product roadmap decisions
  • Monitor industry trends, regulatory shifts, and competitive dynamics
  • Contribute to industry thought leadership, client events, and external positioning


All About You

  • Deep expertise in each the industry verticals within the portfolio under management, including business models, economics, and data-driven decisioning
  • Proven experience owning or contributing to P&L management, revenue growth, or commercial portfolios
  • Strong background in analytics, AI, strategy consulting, and/or data-driven products
  • Comfortable engaging senior executives and shaping complex commercial discussions
  • Ability to operate effectively in a matrixed organisation with shared ownership models
  • Strong judgment around scalability vs bespoke solutions
  • Collaborative leadership style with experience leading and nurturing high-performing teams
  • Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred


Corporate Security Responsibility

All Activities Involving Access To Mastercard Assets, Information, And Networks Comes With An Inherent Risk To The Organization And, Therefore, It Is Expected That Every Person Working For, Or On Behalf Of, Mastercard Is Responsible For Information Security And Must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.


Corporate Security Responsibility

All Activities Involving Access To Mastercard Assets, Information, And Networks Comes With An Inherent Risk To The Organization And, Therefore, It Is Expected That Every Person Working For, Or On Behalf Of, Mastercard Is Responsible For Information Security And Must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Director, Product Management-1
description: About UsVisa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.Job DescriptionAs a Director of Product Development, the professional will be responsible for leading the Product Development team for Debit / Credit Processing in CEMEA, ensuring compliance with Central Europe, Middle East and Africa regulatory obligations within Pismo’s platform. This includes monitoring established rules and changes in the regulatory landscape and relevant bodies, as well as tracking industry standards, market trends, and technological innovations. The role also involves mapping needs, insights, and feedback from current clients and prospects.Key ResponsibilitiesOversee the development of Debit / Credit Card Products aligned with Central Europe, Middle East and Africa regulatory standards and compliance requirements. Continuously monitor regulatory changes and industry benchmarks to ensure product compliance and competitiveness. Collaborate with engineering and business development teams to evaluate implementation approaches for the product roadmap (build vs. partner). Drive cross-functional collaboration with other teams to deliver integrated solutions. Ensure product quality, including non-functional aspects such as integrity, resilience, and scalability. Understand and anticipate customer needs and market dynamics to inform product strategy. Apply critical thinking and articulate improvements in processes and products. This is a remote position. A remote position does not require job duties be performed within proximity of a Visa office location. Remote positions may be required to be present at a Visa office with scheduled notice.Visa requires at least 3 days in office, expectations of these days will be confirmed by your Hiring Manager.QualificationsBasic Qualifications: 10 or more years of work experience with a Bachelor’s Degree or at least 8 years of work experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters/ MBA/JD/MD) or at least 3 years of work experience with a PhD Preferred Qualifications: 12 or more years of work experience with a Bachelor’s Degree or 8-10 years of experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD) or 6+ years of work experience with a PhD 8+ years of experience with CEMEA regulatory standards and compliance frameworks, regional regulators, driving improvements and developing new Credit and Debit Card Processing products. 8+ years in Product Development and Management, specializing in the design and implementation of Credit Card Processing solutions for CEMEA financial institutions. 5 years as Product Director, leading Tribes and Squads focused on the development and support of Issuer Processing Credit and Debit Card Products. Deep understanding of the CEMEA financial market, including product standards, business and regulatory aspects, industry trends, benchmarks, and evolving client needs. Expertise in API integration and microservices architecture within Cloud Computing environments. Proven proficiency in product research and analysis, ensuring data-driven decisions and market alignment. Strong foundation in Regulatory Compliance and Industry Standards, primarily within the CEMEA financial ecosystem. Mandatory experience managing software engineering teams, fostering collaboration and technical excellence. Fintech experience (desirable). Familiarity with Agile methodologies (desirable)Visa is an EEO EmployerQualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.
descriptionHtml:
About Us

Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.

At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.

Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.

Job Description

As a Director of Product Development, the professional will be responsible for leading the Product Development team for Debit / Credit Processing in CEMEA, ensuring compliance with Central Europe, Middle East and Africa regulatory obligations within Pismo’s platform. This includes monitoring established rules and changes in the regulatory landscape and relevant bodies, as well as tracking industry standards, market trends, and technological innovations. The role also involves mapping needs, insights, and feedback from current clients and prospects.

Key Responsibilities

  • Oversee the development of Debit / Credit Card Products aligned with Central Europe, Middle East and Africa regulatory standards and compliance requirements.
  • Continuously monitor regulatory changes and industry benchmarks to ensure product compliance and competitiveness.
  • Collaborate with engineering and business development teams to evaluate implementation approaches for the product roadmap (build vs. partner).
  • Drive cross-functional collaboration with other teams to deliver integrated solutions.
  • Ensure product quality, including non-functional aspects such as integrity, resilience, and scalability.
  • Understand and anticipate customer needs and market dynamics to inform product strategy.
  • Apply critical thinking and articulate improvements in processes and products.

This is a remote position. A remote position does not require job duties be performed within proximity of a Visa office location. Remote positions may be required to be present at a Visa office with scheduled notice.

Visa requires at least 3 days in office, expectations of these days will be confirmed by your Hiring Manager.

Qualifications

Basic Qualifications: 10 or more years of work experience with a Bachelor’s Degree or at least 8 years of work experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters/ MBA/JD/MD) or at least 3 years of work experience with a PhD Preferred Qualifications: 12 or more years of work experience with a Bachelor’s Degree or 8-10 years of experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD) or 6+ years of work experience with a PhD 8+ years of experience with CEMEA regulatory standards and compliance frameworks, regional regulators, driving improvements and developing new Credit and Debit Card Processing products. 8+ years in Product Development and Management, specializing in the design and implementation of Credit Card Processing solutions for CEMEA financial institutions. 5 years as Product Director, leading Tribes and Squads focused on the development and support of Issuer Processing Credit and Debit Card Products. Deep understanding of the CEMEA financial market, including product standards, business and regulatory aspects, industry trends, benchmarks, and evolving client needs. Expertise in API integration and microservices architecture within Cloud Computing environments. Proven proficiency in product research and analysis, ensuring data-driven decisions and market alignment. Strong foundation in Regulatory Compliance and Industry Standards, primarily within the CEMEA financial ecosystem. Mandatory experience managing software engineering teams, fostering collaboration and technical excellence. Fintech experience (desirable). Familiarity with Agile methodologies (desirable)

Visa is an EEO Employer

Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.

title: Director Product Management Data & Analytics Platform
description: We are seeking a Director Product Management to join our global Sabre Enterprise Data, Analytics & AI Platform team, where innovation knows no borders. This team plays a pivotal role in driving the development, innovation and governance of our advanced data, analytics and AI platforms. With an inclusive culture that embraces a flexible work environment, we foster a spirit of collaboration fueled by boldness, curiosity and commitment that empowers us all to win together.As Director Product Management, you will lead and expand our portfolio, ensuring strategic growth and excellence across the organization. You will be responsible for ensuring our data, analytics and AI platform is robust, scalable and compliant with global data governance and regulatory standards. The successful candidate will design and implement policies and processes that uphold data integrity, security, privacy and ethical AI practices, maintaining stakeholder trust and meeting evolving compliance requirements.ResponsibilitiesLead the strategic direction and roadmap for Sabre enterprise data, analytics and AI platform, ensuring alignment with business objectives and customer needs.Drive the development, innovation and governance of advanced data, analytics and AI solutions, maintaining industry-leading standards for scalability, security and compliance.Design and implement policies and processes that uphold data integrity, security, privacy and ethical AI practices, ensuring compliance with global data governance and regulatory standards.Lead and mentor a global team of product managers, fostering a collaborative and high-performance culture across multiple regions.Drive alignment of global product management efforts with corporate strategy, ensuring consistent execution and delivery of platform initiatives worldwide.Preferred Qualifications And EducationOver 10 years of professional experience in product management and technical roles within data, analytics and AI platform environments.Demonstrated ability to lead and mentor a global team of product managers across multiple regions.Proven track record of designing and implementing policies for data integrity, security, privacy and ethical AI practices.Expertise in translating high-level platform and governance visions into actionable strategies and roadmaps.Hands-on experience with Agile methodologies, Rally and Product Quality Assurance (QA).Proficiency in SQL queries and datalake management.Strong communication skills with the capacity to present complex technical concepts to business units and executives.Bonus: Extensive knowledge of cloud technologies with a specific preference for Google Cloud certification.Bonus: Solid understanding of the travel industry and its data-driven business processes.BenefitsBenefits are not one-size fits all, which is why we go beyond the traditional medical and financial benefits to offer perks that promote total well-being. We offer a comprehensive package designed to help you shift into your best self:Competitive pay and performance-based bonusesFlexible work optionsComprehensive healthcare coverageGenerous PTO and holidaysStrong retirement planning supportFamily-friendly benefitsProfessional development opportunities
descriptionHtml:
We are seeking a Director Product Management to join our global Sabre Enterprise Data, Analytics & AI Platform team, where innovation knows no borders. This team plays a pivotal role in driving the development, innovation and governance of our advanced data, analytics and AI platforms. With an inclusive culture that embraces a flexible work environment, we foster a spirit of collaboration fueled by boldness, curiosity and commitment that empowers us all to win together.

As Director Product Management, you will lead and expand our portfolio, ensuring strategic growth and excellence across the organization. You will be responsible for ensuring our data, analytics and AI platform is robust, scalable and compliant with global data governance and regulatory standards. The successful candidate will design and implement policies and processes that uphold data integrity, security, privacy and ethical AI practices, maintaining stakeholder trust and meeting evolving compliance requirements.

Responsibilities

  • Lead the strategic direction and roadmap for Sabre enterprise data, analytics and AI platform, ensuring alignment with business objectives and customer needs.
  • Drive the development, innovation and governance of advanced data, analytics and AI solutions, maintaining industry-leading standards for scalability, security and compliance.
  • Design and implement policies and processes that uphold data integrity, security, privacy and ethical AI practices, ensuring compliance with global data governance and regulatory standards.
  • Lead and mentor a global team of product managers, fostering a collaborative and high-performance culture across multiple regions.
  • Drive alignment of global product management efforts with corporate strategy, ensuring consistent execution and delivery of platform initiatives worldwide.

Preferred Qualifications And Education

  • Over 10 years of professional experience in product management and technical roles within data, analytics and AI platform environments.
  • Demonstrated ability to lead and mentor a global team of product managers across multiple regions.
  • Proven track record of designing and implementing policies for data integrity, security, privacy and ethical AI practices.
  • Expertise in translating high-level platform and governance visions into actionable strategies and roadmaps.
  • Hands-on experience with Agile methodologies, Rally and Product Quality Assurance (QA).
  • Proficiency in SQL queries and datalake management.
  • Strong communication skills with the capacity to present complex technical concepts to business units and executives.
  • Bonus: Extensive knowledge of cloud technologies with a specific preference for Google Cloud certification.
  • Bonus: Solid understanding of the travel industry and its data-driven business processes.

Benefits

Benefits are not one-size fits all, which is why we go beyond the traditional medical and financial benefits to offer perks that promote total well-being. We offer a comprehensive package designed to help you shift into your best self:

  • Competitive pay and performance-based bonuses
  • Flexible work options
  • Comprehensive healthcare coverage
  • Generous PTO and holidays
  • Strong retirement planning support
  • Family-friendly benefits
  • Professional development opportunities
