descriptionHtml:
About Learneo
Learneo is a platform of builder-driven businesses, including Course Hero, CliffsNotes, LitCharts, Quillbot, Symbolab, and Scribbr, all united around a shared mission of supercharging productivity and learning for everyone. We attract and scale high growth businesses built and run by visionary entrepreneurs. Each team innovates independently but has a unique opportunity to collaborate, experiment, and grow together, and they are supported by centralized corporate operations functions, including HR, Finance and Legal.

Role Overview
We are looking for a strategic, high-ownership Director of Organic Growth to lead and scale QuillBot’s organic acquisition engine across all major discovery surfaces. This role goes well beyond traditional SEO—you will own the full spectrum of organic growth, including Search Engine Optimization (SEO), App Store Optimization (ASO), Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), and Extension Store visibility (Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, and others). As a Director, you will set the vision, build and manage the roadmap, and lead the existing SEO & Organic Growth team. You’ll partner closely with Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, and International teams to embed organic growth into QuillBot’s product and go-to-market strategy. Your work will directly influence organic traffic, sales, install volume, revenue, and brand visibility on a global scale.

Responsibilities

  • Strategy & Leadership
    • Organic Growth Strategy: Define and own the overarching organic growth strategy spanning SEO, ASO, GEO, and Extension Store optimization. Set quarterly and annual OKRs tied to impressions, clicks, MAU, installs, and sales. 
    • Team Leadership: Manage, mentor, and develop the SEO & Organic Growth team. Foster a culture of experimentation, accountability, and continuous learning. Identify and hire for key capability gaps as needed.
    • Roadmap Ownership: Build and maintain a prioritized organic growth roadmap. Balance quick wins with longer-term strategic bets. Present plans and progress to senior leadership, including the CEO.
    • Cross-Functional Partnership: Serve as the organic growth voice in cross-functional planning with AI, Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, Marketing Operations, and International Expansion teams. Ensure organic considerations are embedded in product launches, site architecture decisions, and content strategy.
    • Product Roadmap Influence: Act as a key influencer of the Product roadmap by surfacing organic growth opportunities, user intent data, and search landscape insights that shape feature prioritization, new product bets, and go-to-market timing.
  • Search Engine Optimization (SEO)
    • SEO Program Ownership: Own end-to-end SEO performance across QuillBot’s web properties. Drive strategy across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO to secure and defend top positions for high-value keywords.
    • Technical SEO: Lead technical audits and partner with Engineering to resolve crawlability, indexation, Core Web Vitals, site speed, structured data, and mobile-responsiveness issues at scale.
    • Content & Keyword Strategy: Oversee keyword research, content gap analysis, and on-page optimization. Collaborate with the Content team to ensure every page is strategically targeted and continuously improved.
    • Link Building & Authority: Direct link-building and digital PR strategies to grow domain authority. Evaluate backlink quality and build partnerships with authoritative sites.
    • Algorithm Resilience: Monitor Google algorithm updates, diagnose ranking fluctuations, and quickly adapt strategies to protect and grow organic traffic.
  • Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO)
    • GEO Strategy: Develop and execute a strategy to maximize QuillBot’s visibility in AI-generated answers and citations across generative search experiences (e.g., Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, etc).
    • Content for LLMs: Work with Content and Product to structure information, schema markup, and authoritative content so that generative engines accurately surface and recommend QuillBot.
    • Measurement & Experimentation: Define GEO-specific metrics, build tracking frameworks, and run experiments to understand how generative surfaces drive traffic and conversions.
    • Competitive Intelligence: Monitor how competitors are cited in generative answers and identify opportunities to increase QuillBot’s share of voice.
  • App Store Optimisation (ASO)
    • ASO Program Ownership: Own QuillBot’s organic discoverability and conversion rates in the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.
    • Listing Optimization: Optimize app titles, subtitles, descriptions, keywords, screenshots, and preview videos to improve search rankings and download conversion rates.
    • Ratings & Reviews: Develop strategies to increase the volume and quality of positive ratings and reviews, and work with Product to address recurring user feedback themes.
    • A/B Testing: Design and run store listing experiments to continuously improve conversion. Leverage tools like Google Play Experiments and third-party ASO platforms.
    • Market-Specific ASO: Partner with the International Expansion team to localize and optimize listings for priority markets.
  • Extension Store Optimisation
    • Extension Visibility: Own QuillBot’s presence and discoverability in the Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, Microsoft Edge Add-ons, and Firefox Add-ons.
    • Listing & CRO: Optimize extension titles, descriptions, screenshots, and feature graphics. Improve install conversion rates through iterative testing.
    • Ratings & Review Strategy: Drive rating volume and quality for extensions. Coordinate with Product and Support to address negative feedback and reduce churn.
    • Cross-Channel Synergy: Align extension store strategy with SEO and ASO efforts to create a unified organic discovery experience across web, app, and extension surfaces.
  • Analytics, Reporting & Insights
    • Performance Measurement: Build and maintain dashboards tracking organic performance across all channels (SEO, ASO, GEO, Extension Stores). Define KPIs and ensure the team is data-driven in decision-making.
    • Executive Reporting: Deliver regular performance updates and strategic insights to senior leadership. Translate complex organic data into clear narratives with actionable recommendations.
    • Tool Stack: Evaluate and manage the organic growth tool stack (e.g., Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Sensor Tower, Screaming Frog, etc).

Qualifications

  • 8–10 years of progressive experience in SEO and/or organic growth, with at least 3 years in a people-management role.
  • Global experience: demonstrated ability to drive organic growth across multiple markets, regions, and languages—not limited to English-speaking audiences.
  • Demonstrated success managing organic growth for a high-traffic website (+20M monthly active users) and a consumer app with significant install volume.
  • Proven track record of securing and defending #1 positions for competitive keywords
  • Proven ability to build organic visibility from zero for new product launches in highly competitive markets — taking a product from no search presence to top-ranking positions against established incumbents.
  • Deep expertise across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO, with a strong grasp of how search engines crawl, index, and rank content.
  • Hands-on experience with ASO for iOS and/or Android apps, including keyword optimization, listing experiments, and review management.
  • Strong understanding of the generative AI search landscape and emerging GEO best practices.
  • Proficiency with industry tools: Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, and at least one ASO platform (preferably Sensor Tower).
  • Experience working with CMS platforms (e.g., WordPress) and comfort with basic HTML/CSS.
  • Excellent communicator who can present data-driven strategies to senior leadership, including C-level stakeholders.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field.

Bonus Points

  • Experience at a SaaS, EdTech, or consumer-tech company with a freemium or subscription business model.
  • Hands-on experience with multilingual and multi-regional SEO and ASO strategies.
  • Familiarity with structured data, schema markup, and Knowledge Graph optimization.
  • Experience with programmatic SEO or large-scale page generation strategies.
  • Experience optimizing browser extension listings in the Chrome Web Store or equivalent platforms.
  • Background in conversion rate optimization (CRO) as it relates to organic landing pages and store listings.
  • Comfortable working across time zones in a fully remote, globally distributed team.

Why Join QuillBot?

  • Ownership & Impact: Take full ownership of organic growth across every discovery surface and directly influence QuillBot’s trajectory.
  • Scope & Challenge: Lead a function that spans SEO, ASO, GEO, and Extension Stores—a rare opportunity to shape organic strategy holistically.
  • Collaborative Culture: Work with a passionate, innovative team across Content, Marketing, Product, and Engineering.
  • Remote Flexibility: Enjoy the flexibility of working remotely with flexible hours to balance professional and personal life.
  • Professional Growth: QuillBot values learning and development, offering opportunities for skill enhancement and career advancement.

Benefits & Perks

  • Competitive salary and annual bonus
  • Medical coverage
  • Life and accidental insurance
  • Vacation & leaves of absence (menstrual, flexible, special, and more!)
  • Developmental opportunities through education & developmental reimbursements & professional workshops
  • Maternity & parental leave
  • Hybrid & remote model with flexible working hours
  • On-site & remote company events throughout the year
  • Tech & WFH stipends & new hire allowances
  • Employee referral program
  • Premium access to QuillBot

*Benefits and benefit amounts differ by region. A comprehensive list applicable to your region will be provided in your interview process. 

Research shows that candidates from underrepresented backgrounds often don't apply for roles if they don't meet all the criteria. We strongly encourage you to apply if you're interested: we'd love to learn how you can amplify our team with your unique experience!

This role is eligible for hire in India.

We are a virtual-first company and have employees dispersed throughout the United States, Canada, India, Israel, Germany, and the Netherlands. We have a market-based pay structure that varies by location. The base pay for this position is dependent on multiple factors, including candidate experience and expertise, and may vary from the amounts listed. You may also be eligible to participate in our bonus program and may be offered benefits, and other types of compensation.

#Learneo

Equal ​Employment Opportunity Statement ​(EEO)

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity and ​inclusion within our company.  ​We will consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender ​identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, ​veteran status, or ​ability status. We will ensure that individuals who are differently abled are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment as provided to other applicants or employees. Please contact us to request accommodation.

Today, we help over 56 million people around the world create great things. Our AI-powered tools help you think clearly, communicate effectively, and create beautifully—across every platform, in any format, at any skill level. If you're passionate about using technology to make the path from inspiration to execution more accessible, intentional, and relevant, come join us. descriptionHtml:
About Us
QuillBot was founded in 2017 with a mission to help students and professionals strengthen their writing. Today, we help over 56 million people around the world create great things. Whether you're writing, designing, coding, or collaborating, QuillBot is a place where anyone can create at the speed of thought. Our AI-powered tools help you think clearly, communicate effectively, and create beautifully—across every platform, in any format, at any skill level. If you're passionate about using technology to make the path from inspiration to execution more accessible, intentional, and relevant, come join us.

Role Overview
We are looking for a strategic, high-ownership Director of Organic Growth to lead and scale QuillBot’s organic acquisition engine across all major discovery surfaces. This role goes well beyond traditional SEO—you will own the full spectrum of organic growth, including Search Engine Optimization (SEO), App Store Optimization (ASO), Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), and Extension Store visibility (Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, and others). As a Director, you will set the vision, build and manage the roadmap, and lead the existing SEO & Organic Growth team. You’ll partner closely with Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, and International teams to embed organic growth into QuillBot’s product and go-to-market strategy. Your work will directly influence organic traffic, sales, install volume, revenue, and brand visibility on a global scale.

Responsibilities

  • Strategy & Leadership
    • Organic Growth Strategy: Define and own the overarching organic growth strategy spanning SEO, ASO, GEO, and Extension Store optimization. Set quarterly and annual OKRs tied to impressions, clicks, MAU, installs, and sales. 
    • Team Leadership: Manage, mentor, and develop the SEO & Organic Growth team. Foster a culture of experimentation, accountability, and continuous learning. Identify and hire for key capability gaps as needed.
    • Roadmap Ownership: Build and maintain a prioritized organic growth roadmap. Balance quick wins with longer-term strategic bets. Present plans and progress to senior leadership, including the CEO.
    • Cross-Functional Partnership: Serve as the organic growth voice in cross-functional planning with AI, Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, Marketing Operations, and International Expansion teams. Ensure organic considerations are embedded in product launches, site architecture decisions, and content strategy.
    • Product Roadmap Influence: Act as a key influencer of the Product roadmap by surfacing organic growth opportunities, user intent data, and search landscape insights that shape feature prioritization, new product bets, and go-to-market timing.
  • Search Engine Optimization (SEO)
    • SEO Program Ownership: Own end-to-end SEO performance across QuillBot’s web properties. Drive strategy across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO to secure and defend top positions for high-value keywords.
    • Technical SEO: Lead technical audits and partner with Engineering to resolve crawlability, indexation, Core Web Vitals, site speed, structured data, and mobile-responsiveness issues at scale.
    • Content & Keyword Strategy: Oversee keyword research, content gap analysis, and on-page optimization. Collaborate with the Content team to ensure every page is strategically targeted and continuously improved.
    • Link Building & Authority: Direct link-building and digital PR strategies to grow domain authority. Evaluate backlink quality and build partnerships with authoritative sites.
    • Algorithm Resilience: Monitor Google algorithm updates, diagnose ranking fluctuations, and quickly adapt strategies to protect and grow organic traffic.
  • Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO)
    • GEO Strategy: Develop and execute a strategy to maximize QuillBot’s visibility in AI-generated answers and citations across generative search experiences (e.g., Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, etc).
    • Content for LLMs: Work with Content and Product to structure information, schema markup, and authoritative content so that generative engines accurately surface and recommend QuillBot.
    • Measurement & Experimentation: Define GEO-specific metrics, build tracking frameworks, and run experiments to understand how generative surfaces drive traffic and conversions.
    • Competitive Intelligence: Monitor how competitors are cited in generative answers and identify opportunities to increase QuillBot’s share of voice.
  • App Store Optimisation (ASO)
    • ASO Program Ownership: Own QuillBot’s organic discoverability and conversion rates in the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.
    • Listing Optimization: Optimize app titles, subtitles, descriptions, keywords, screenshots, and preview videos to improve search rankings and download conversion rates.
    • Ratings & Reviews: Develop strategies to increase the volume and quality of positive ratings and reviews, and work with Product to address recurring user feedback themes.
    • A/B Testing: Design and run store listing experiments to continuously improve conversion. Leverage tools like Google Play Experiments and third-party ASO platforms.
    • Market-Specific ASO: Partner with the International Expansion team to localize and optimize listings for priority markets.
  • Extension Store Optimisation
    • Extension Visibility: Own QuillBot’s presence and discoverability in the Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, Microsoft Edge Add-ons, and Firefox Add-ons.
    • Listing & CRO: Optimize extension titles, descriptions, screenshots, and feature graphics. Improve install conversion rates through iterative testing.
    • Ratings & Review Strategy: Drive rating volume and quality for extensions. Coordinate with Product and Support to address negative feedback and reduce churn.
    • Cross-Channel Synergy: Align extension store strategy with SEO and ASO efforts to create a unified organic discovery experience across web, app, and extension surfaces.
  • Analytics, Reporting & Insights
    • Performance Measurement: Build and maintain dashboards tracking organic performance across all channels (SEO, ASO, GEO, Extension Stores). Define KPIs and ensure the team is data-driven in decision-making.
    • Executive Reporting: Deliver regular performance updates and strategic insights to senior leadership. Translate complex organic data into clear narratives with actionable recommendations.
    • Tool Stack: Evaluate and manage the organic growth tool stack (e.g., Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Sensor Tower, Screaming Frog, etc).

Qualifications

  • 8–10 years of progressive experience in SEO and/or organic growth, with at least 3 years in a people-management role.
  • Global experience: demonstrated ability to drive organic growth across multiple markets, regions, and languages—not limited to English-speaking audiences.
  • Demonstrated success managing organic growth for a high-traffic website (+20M monthly active users) and a consumer app with significant install volume.
  • Proven track record of securing and defending #1 positions for competitive keywords
  • Proven ability to build organic visibility from zero for new product launches in highly competitive markets — taking a product from no search presence to top-ranking positions against established incumbents.
  • Deep expertise across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO, with a strong grasp of how search engines crawl, index, and rank content.
  • Hands-on experience with ASO for iOS and/or Android apps, including keyword optimization, listing experiments, and review management.
  • Strong understanding of the generative AI search landscape and emerging GEO best practices.
  • Proficiency with industry tools: Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, and at least one ASO platform (preferably Sensor Tower).
  • Experience working with CMS platforms (e.g., WordPress) and comfort with basic HTML/CSS.
  • Excellent communicator who can present data-driven strategies to senior leadership, including C-level stakeholders.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field.

Bonus Points

  • Experience at a SaaS, EdTech, or consumer-tech company with a freemium or subscription business model.
  • Hands-on experience with multilingual and multi-regional SEO and ASO strategies.
  • Familiarity with structured data, schema markup, and Knowledge Graph optimization.
  • Experience with programmatic SEO or large-scale page generation strategies.
  • Experience optimizing browser extension listings in the Chrome Web Store or equivalent platforms.
  • Background in conversion rate optimization (CRO) as it relates to organic landing pages and store listings.
  • Comfortable working across time zones in a fully remote, globally distributed team.

Why Join QuillBot?

  • Ownership & Impact: Take full ownership of organic growth across every discovery surface and directly influence QuillBot’s trajectory.
  • Scope & Challenge: Lead a function that spans SEO, ASO, GEO, and Extension Stores—a rare opportunity to shape organic strategy holistically.
  • Collaborative Culture: Work with a passionate, innovative team across Content, Marketing, Product, and Engineering.
  • Remote Flexibility: Enjoy the flexibility of working remotely with flexible hours to balance professional and personal life.
  • Professional Growth: QuillBot values learning and development, offering opportunities for skill enhancement and career advancement.

Benefits & Perks

  • Competitive salary and annual bonus
  • Medical coverage
  • Life and accidental insurance
  • Vacation & leaves of absence (menstrual, flexible, special, and more!)
  • Developmental opportunities through education & developmental reimbursements & professional workshops
  • Maternity & parental leave
  • Hybrid & remote model with flexible working hours
  • On-site & remote company events throughout the year
  • Tech & WFH stipends & new hire allowances
  • Employee referral program
  • Premium access to QuillBot

*Benefits and benefit amounts differ by region. A comprehensive list applicable to your region will be provided in your interview process. 

Research shows that candidates from underrepresented backgrounds often don't apply for roles if they don't meet all the criteria. We strongly encourage you to apply if you're interested: we'd love to learn how you can amplify our team with your unique experience!

This role is eligible for hire in India.

We are a virtual-first company with employees across the United States, Canada, India, Germany, and the Netherlands. Our compensation approach is market-based and varies by location while ensuring fairness, consistency, and transparency.

The base pay for this role is determined by several factors, including:

  • Relevant professional experience
  • Skills and expertise
  • Scope and responsibilities of the role
  • Internal equity considerations 

In addition to base salary, total compensation may include bonus, equity, and benefits. Eligibility depends on role, level, and location.

We are committed to equal pay for equal work, and all compensation decisions are made using consistent, gender-neutral criteria in alignment with pay transparency requirements

#QuillBot

Equal ​Employment Opportunity Statement ​(EEO)
We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity and ​inclusion within our company.  ​We will consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender ​identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, ​veteran status, or ​ability status. We will ensure that individuals who are differently abled are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment as provided to other applicants or employees. Please contact us to request accommodation.

descriptionHtml:
At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.

Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.

Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.

Payments Director

The Company

eBay Inc. is a global ecommerce leader that connects over 134 million people around the world and builds communities to create economic opportunity for all. The company participates in190 markets around the world, and in 2024 enabled more than $75 billion of gross merchandise volume. eBay’s Marketplace platforms, including its global properties and its off-platform businesses in Japan create one of the world's largest and most vibrant marketplaces for discovering great value and unique selection. eBay's payment business also powers tens of billions of dollars of online transactions while providing financial services such as seller lending, buyer and seller FX, and multiple payment solutions.

For more information about the Company and its global portfolio of online brands, visit www.ebayinc.com

eBay’s strategic vision is to become the best global marketplace for enthusiast buyers and sellers. Looking for a purpose-driven company that puts the customer at the heart of everything? A culture that encourages people to think big and be curious, to act with urgency and integrity and to be fearlessly inclusive? That’s who we are. If this sounds compelling, join eBay!

Key Responsibilities

  • Technical Vision & Strategy:
    • Define and drive the long-term technical vision, roadmap, and architecture for the engineering organization.
    • Align engineering strategy with overall business goals and product vision.
    • Evaluate and recommend new technologies, tools, and methodologies.
    • Ensure technical decisions support scalability, reliability, security, and maintainability.

  • Team Leadership & Management:
    • Lead, mentor, and grow multiple Engineering Managers and/or Team Leads.
    • Oversee the hiring, onboarding, performance management, and career development of engineering staff (including managers and senior ICs).
    • Foster a high-performing, collaborative, innovative, and inclusive engineering culture.
    • Build and maintain team morale, engagement, and retention.

  • Delivery & Execution:
    • Oversee the planning, execution, and timely delivery of complex software projects across multiple teams.
    • Ensure teams adhere to best practices (coding standards, code reviews, testing, CI/CD).
    • Manage engineering budgets, resource allocation, and capacity planning.
    • Track key performance indicators (KPIs) related to delivery velocity, quality, and system health

  • Process & Operational Excellence:
    • Establish, optimize, and standardize engineering processes (SDLC, Agile/Scrum/Kanban, release management, incident response).
    • Drive continuous improvement in engineering efficiency, productivity, and quality.
    • Implement and monitor metrics to measure team and organizational performance.

  • Cross-Functional Collaboration:
    • Partner closely with Product Management, Design, Data Science, Security, and IT/Operations to define requirements, prioritize work, and ensure successful product delivery.
    • Collaborate with senior leadership (Sr. Directors, other Directors, Executives) on strategic initiatives and company-wide goals.
    • Represent the engineering organization in cross-functional meetings and strategic discussions.

  • Technical Oversight & Quality:
    • Provide high-level technical guidance and oversight across projects.
    • Champion software quality, best practices, and operational reliability (e.g., SLOs, SLIs).
    • Ensure systems are designed for scalability, performance, and cost-efficiency.
    • Manage technical debt and advocate for necessary refactoring/infrastructure investments.

  • Resource Management:
    • Growing your team through coaching, mentoring
    • Forecast hiring needs and participate in recruitment strategy.
Job Requirements

  • 10+ years of software engineering experience, with 5+ years in direct people management and 3+ years managing other managers.
  • Master's degree in Computer Science, or Bachelor's degree with equivalent experience
  • Proven ability to lead, inspire, mentor, and grow large engineering teams and managers. Strong coaching skills.
  • Significant hands-on technical background (software development, architecture, systems design). Ability to understand complex technical challenges and trade-offs deeply, even if not coding daily.
  • Ability to set technical vision, translate business strategy into engineering execution, and make long-term architectural decisions.
  • Proven track record of shipping complex, high-quality software products at scale. Expertise in Agile methodologies.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills. Ability to articulate complex technical concepts to both technical and non-technical audiences (including executives).
  • Excellent interpersonal and stakeholder management skills. Ability to build strong cross-functional partnerships.
  • Strong analytical and critical thinking skills. Ability to navigate ambiguity and make sound decisions under pressure.
  • Experience establishing processes, metrics, and driving continuous improvement.
  • Familiarity with AI/Machine Learning solutions to enhance service capabilities.


Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in ECommerce and Payments/Fintech industry
  • Experience scaling engineering organizations during rapid growth


Please see the Talent Privacy Notice for information regarding how eBay handles your personal data collected when you use the eBay Careers website or apply for a job with eBay.

eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.

The eBay Jobs website uses cookies to enhance your experience. By continuing to browse the site, you agree to our use of cookies. Visit our Privacy Center for more information.

title: Director of Product Design
description: About EthosEthos is a leading life insurance technology company on a mission to protect families by democratizing access to life insurance and empowering agents at scale. With its robust three-sided technology platform, Ethos is transforming the life insurance experience for consumers, agents, and carriers alike. Ethos offers instant, accessible products and a seamless online process that requires no medical exams and just a few health questions; it eliminates traditional barriers, making it easier than ever for everyone to protect their families. Ethos is redefining how life insurance is bought, sold, and underwritten.About The RoleWe’re looking for a Director of Product Design to lead and elevate design for Ethos’s core business — the end-to-end journey that takes customers from landing page to policy activation and beyond. You’ll be responsible for defining how Ethos helps people navigate complex decisions with confidence, clarity, and care.You’ll collaborate with Product, Engineering, Research, and Brand leaders to ensure every user touchpoint is intuitive, efficient, and emotionally resonant with user needs and the brand.This role combines strategic vision, team management, with hands-on design and product building excellence. You’ll champion intentional, intuitive, and engaging design, compelling storytelling, strategic experimentation, and measurable business impact — and will help make Ethos the most trusted and user-friendly life insurance experience in the world.Duties And ResponsibilitiesLead design for the core conversion funnel — covering landing experiences, application flows, underwriting, checkout, onboarding, and policy management.Lead, mentor, and grow a small but mighty team of product designers.Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Research, Marketing, and Brand to deliver cohesive experiences that delight users and align with key business outcomes such as conversion and retention.Raise the bar for the design practice across: design systems and craft, accessibility,decision-making discipline, experimentation, storytelling, leverage of research and data.Partner with adjacent design functions, such as Performance Creative and Brand, to create a consistent, connected user journey across growth and product surfaces.Contribute to create an environment of excellence and growth where team members can do their best work and grow professionally and personally. Qualifications10+ years of product design (UX) experience, including several years leading multi-person design teams.Proven ability to design for complex, decision-heavy user journeys in b2c journeys. industries like fintech, healthtech, or insurtech.Strong understanding of conversion optimization, user motivation, and behavior design principles.Demonstrated experience turning ambiguity into clarity — structuring complex information and simplifying it into elegant, actionable flows.Skilled at storytelling and stakeholder communication — you make design rationale clear, measurable, and compelling.A track record of positive impact to product quality and design culture.A portfolio showcasing strong visual and interaction design craft alongside compelling impact delivered for past businesses and their customers. Don’t meet every single requirement? If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. At Ethos we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace.We are an equal opportunity employer.. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. Pursuant to the SF Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider employment for qualified applicants with arrests and conviction records.To learn more about what information we collect and how it may be used, please refer to our California Candidate Privacy Notice.Recruitment Notice: Please be aware of recruitment scams. All legitimate communication from our team will only come from email addresses ending in @ethos.com or @getethos.com.We will never ask for payment, banking details, or sensitive personal information during the hiring process. If you are contacted by someone claiming to represent us from a different email address, please treat it as fraudulent.

About Ethos

Ethos is a leading life insurance technology company on a mission to protect families by democratizing access to life insurance and empowering agents at scale. With its robust three-sided technology platform, Ethos is transforming the life insurance experience for consumers, agents, and carriers alike. Ethos offers instant, accessible products and a seamless online process that requires no medical exams and just a few health questions; it eliminates traditional barriers, making it easier than ever for everyone to protect their families. Ethos is redefining how life insurance is bought, sold, and underwritten.

About The Role

We’re looking for a Director of Product Design to lead and elevate design for Ethos’s core business — the end-to-end journey that takes customers from landing page to policy activation and beyond. You’ll be responsible for defining how Ethos helps people navigate complex decisions with confidence, clarity, and care.

You’ll collaborate with Product, Engineering, Research, and Brand leaders to ensure every user touchpoint is intuitive, efficient, and emotionally resonant with user needs and the brand.

This role combines strategic vision, team management, with hands-on design and product building excellence. You’ll champion intentional, intuitive, and engaging design, compelling storytelling, strategic experimentation, and measurable business impact — and will help make Ethos the most trusted and user-friendly life insurance experience in the world.

Duties And Responsibilities

  • Lead design for the core conversion funnel — covering landing experiences, application flows, underwriting, checkout, onboarding, and policy management.
  • Lead, mentor, and grow a small but mighty team of product designers.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Research, Marketing, and Brand to deliver cohesive experiences that delight users and align with key business outcomes such as conversion and retention.
  • Raise the bar for the design practice across: design systems and craft, accessibility,decision-making discipline, experimentation, storytelling, leverage of research and data.
  • Partner with adjacent design functions, such as Performance Creative and Brand, to create a consistent, connected user journey across growth and product surfaces.
  • Contribute to create an environment of excellence and growth where team members can do their best work and grow professionally and personally.

Qualifications

  • 10+ years of product design (UX) experience, including several years leading multi-person design teams.
  • Proven ability to design for complex, decision-heavy user journeys in b2c journeys. industries like fintech, healthtech, or insurtech.
  • Strong understanding of conversion optimization, user motivation, and behavior design principles.
  • Demonstrated experience turning ambiguity into clarity — structuring complex information and simplifying it into elegant, actionable flows.
  • Skilled at storytelling and stakeholder communication — you make design rationale clear, measurable, and compelling.
  • A track record of positive impact to product quality and design culture.
  • A portfolio showcasing strong visual and interaction design craft alongside compelling impact delivered for past businesses and their customers.

Don’t meet every single requirement? If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. At Ethos we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace.

We are an equal opportunity employer.. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. Pursuant to the SF Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider employment for qualified applicants with arrests and conviction records.

To learn more about what information we collect and how it may be used, please refer to our California Candidate Privacy Notice.

Recruitment Notice: Please be aware of recruitment scams. All legitimate communication from our team will only come from email addresses ending in @ethos.com or @getethos.com.

We will never ask for payment, banking details, or sensitive personal information during the hiring process. If you are contacted by someone claiming to represent us from a different email address, please treat it as fraudulent.

descriptionHtml:
Director, Product Management, Data Threat Analytics

Location: Bangalore, India

About The Team

The Product Management team at Rubrik drives Rubrik’s vision forward, ensuring we continue to secure the world’s data by delivering on our market-leading platform to address our customers’ most challenging data management & security needs. Our Product Managers are responsible for guiding multidisciplinary teams towards the goal of shipping high-quality products on time. We wear multiple hats throughout the different stages in the product lifecycle and set the vision for our growing family of products. As advocates for both our users and our business, Product Managers balance varied perspectives and empower their teams to do great work.

About The Role

This role requires a leader who can set a compelling strategy, align diverse teams, and turn complex recovery problems into clear, actionable outcomes.

For this, we are seeking a Director of Product Management - Data Threat Analytics with a strong hybrid and cloud security background to lead a product area (and PMs) focused on detecting and understanding threats against sensitive data in hybrid and cloud environments - with recovery as the end goal. This role owns anomaly detection, threat hunting, monitoring, and sensitive data discovery on backed-up data, and will help reimagine cyber recovery for hybrid and modern cloud architectures.

What You’ll Own

Product Strategy:

  • Define and own the long-term product vision for data threat analytics anchored in hybrid, and cloud-native environments, and recovery-first outcomes.
  • Reimagine how threat signals, anomaly detection, and sensitive data discovery directly inform - blast radius assessment, clean restore point selection and how this feeds into confident cyber recovery workflows
  • Develop a point of view on the evolving hybrid and cloud threat landscape, including ransomware, and misconfigurations - and translate into differentiated product strategy

Execution Excellence

  • Drive end-to-end execution for recovery across matrix organization and own the process from concept validation to GA and customer adoption while holding the upstream teams accountable
  • Break-down ambiguous, multi-year product bets into clear milestones, measurable outcomes, and incremental value
  • Establish strong product operating rhythms - with tight roadmap discipline and backlog hygiene

People & Org Leadership

  • Build, lead and scale a high-performing team of Product Managers
  • Coach PMs to think beyond feature delivery toward - problem framing, systems thinking and recovery outcomes.
  • Develop PM talent with strong focus on structured thinking and crisp communication, executive-ready storytelling and customer-centric results.
  • Foster a culture of high accountability with stakeholders and build teams that move fast, learn, and course-correct.
  • Create clarity in a complex org by defining ownership boundaries, decision-making frameworks, and clear interfaces with engineering, snappable PMs, data science and security research.

Cross-Functional Leadership

  • Act as a cross-functional leader and unifier, aligning product, engineering, data science, security research and go-to-market teams around shared outcomes.
  • Serve as a credible product voice in executive reviews, roadmap discussions, and key customer conversations.
  • Enforce strong boundaries on snappable PM teams for persona, and problem definition and learning to leverage teams for their expertise instead of wanting to do it all
  • Navigate ambiguity and competing priorities by driving alignment
  • Serve as a trusted partner to global stakeholders across US, India and Israel, navigating distributed execution and time-zone collaboration effectively

What We’re Looking For:-

Required Experience

  • 10-15 years in product management, with 5+ years managing PMs.
  • Strong Enterprise PM background
  • Experience in one of the following: Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM), or Cloud Detection & Response (CDR) or DSPM
  • Deep understanding of public cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP), or identity services or SaaS models.
  • Experience building analytics-heavy or detection-driven products at scale.

Strong Signals

  • Has a strong track record of shipping enterprise products to Global 2000s
  • Understands why alert fatigue kills adoption and how to design trusted signals.
  • Comfortable discussing cloud cost models, scale tradeoffs, and multi-tenant architectures.

Bonus (Nice to Have)

  • Exposure to backup, recovery, or resilience platforms.
  • Experience working with data classification, lineage, or access intelligence.
  • Familiarity with ransomware or destructive cloud attacks, database vulnerabilities. identity-based attacks or SaaS vulnerabilities

Join Us in Securing and Accelerating the World's AI Transformation

Rubrik (RBRK), the Security and AI Operations Company, leads at the intersection of data protection, cyber resilience, and enterprise AI acceleration. Rubrik Security Cloud delivers complete cyber resilience by securing, monitoring, and recovering data, identities, and workloads across clouds. Rubrik Agent Cloud accelerates trusted AI agent deployments at scale by monitoring and auditing agentic actions, enforcing real-time guardrails, fine-tuning for accuracy and undoing agentic mistakes.

Linkedin | X (formerly Twitter) | Instagram | Rubrik.com

Inclusion @ Rubrik

At Rubrik, we are dedicated to fostering a culture where people from all backgrounds are valued, feel they belong, and believe they can succeed. Our commitment to inclusion is at the heart of our mission to secure the world’s data.

Our goal is to hire and promote the best talent, regardless of background. We continually review our hiring practices to ensure fairness and strive to create an environment where every employee has equal access to opportunities for growth and excellence. We believe in empowering everyone to bring their authentic selves to work and achieve their fullest potential.

Our inclusion strategy focuses on three core areas of our business and culture:

  • Our Company: We are committed to building a merit-based organization that offers equal access to growth and success for all employees globally. Your potential is limitless here.
  • Our Culture: We strive to create an inclusive atmosphere where individuals from all backgrounds feel a strong sense of belonging, can thrive, and do their best work. Your contributions help us innovate and break boundaries.
  • Our Communities: We are dedicated to expanding our engagement with the communities we operate in, creating opportunities for underrepresented talent and driving greater innovation for our clients. Your impact extends beyond Rubrik, contributing to safer and stronger communities.

Equal Opportunity Employer/Veterans/Disabled

Rubrik is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, or protected veteran status and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability.

Rubrik provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability or genetics. In addition to federal law requirements, Rubrik complies with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training.

Federal law requires employers to provide reasonable accommodation to qualified individuals with disabilities. Please contact us at hr@rubrik.com if you require a reasonable accommodation to apply for a job or to perform your job. Examples of reasonable accommodation include making a change to the application process or work procedures, providing documents in an alternate format, using a sign language interpreter, or using specialized equipment.

EEO IS THE LAW

NOTIFICATION OF EMPLOYEE RIGHTS UNDER FEDERAL LABOR LAWS

descriptionHtml:
PL Operations Manager Location: Bengaluru (Hybrid: 3 days in-office)


Shift: US Business Hours


Company: Product


We are looking for a data-driven PL Operations Leader with 10+ years of experience to manage our workforce and analytical strategy.


The Role

  • Team Management: Lead a team focused on Workforce Planning, Scheduling, and Real-Time Analysis (RTA).
  • Data Visualization: Build and manage advanced dashboards and reports in Salesforce.
  • Strategic Analysis: Drive operational decisions using Excel, Power BI, and Tableau.
  • Stakeholder Management: Act as the primary liaison for US-based customers and leadership.


Key Requirements

  • Experience: 10+ total years, with 2+ years in a people management role.
  • Technical Skills: Advanced proficiency in Salesforce, Power BI, Tableau, and Excel.
  • Communication: Excellent verbal and written skills; experience collaborating with US stakeholders is mandatory.
  • WFM Expertise: Proven background in workforce optimization and real-time monitoring.


Apply Now


If you are a strategic leader ready to drive operations for a global product company, apply today

descriptionHtml:
About Us

HighLevel is an AI-powered business operating system that gives agencies, entrepreneurs and SMBs the infrastructure to build, automate and scale. Today, HighLevel supports SMBs across 150+ countries, fueling community-driven growth rooted in real customer outcomes.

To date, businesses operating on HighLevel have generated over $7 billion in ecosystem value, demonstrating the impact of shared infrastructure at scale. By centralizing conversations, automation and intelligence into one system, we help businesses move faster, reduce complexity and execute efficiently.

Behind the platform, HighLevel powers more than 4 billion API hits and 2.5 billion message events daily. With 250 terabytes of distributed data, 250+ microservices and over 1 million domain names supported, our architecture is built for performance, resilience and long-term scalability.

Our people

With over 2,000 team members across 10+ countries, HighLevel operates as a global, remote-first organization built for speed and ownership. We value initiative, clarity and execution, creating space for ambitious people to build systems that support millions of businesses worldwide. Here, innovation thrives, ideas are celebrated and people come first, no matter where they call home.

Our impact

Every month, HighLevel enables more than 1.5 billion messages, 200 million leads and 20 million conversations for the more than 1 million businesses we support. Behind those numbers are real people building independence, expanding opportunity and creating measurable impact. We’re proud to be a part of that.

Learn more about us on our YouTube Channel or Blog Posts

The Role

We’re looking for a Director of Design, Growth Products to lead and scale design across our most critical growth experiences. This role owns the end-to-end design strategy for key growth surfaces including new user onboarding, product activation, expansion experiences, and cancellation/retention flows .

You will partner closely with Product, Engineering, Data, and Marketing to drive measurable business impact while delivering intuitive, high-quality user experiences. This is a hands-on leadership role for someone who thrives at the intersection of strategy, craft, experimentation, and people leadership .

What You’ll Own

  • Design strategy and execution for growth-focused product areas , including:
  • New user onboarding and first-run experiences
  • Product activation and adoption flows
  • Monetization, upgrade, and retention experiences
  • Cancellation, downgrade, and save flows
  • The design vision and roadmap for growth products, ensuring alignment with company goals and user needs
  • A team of product designers, including hiring, coaching, performance management, and career development
  • Design quality, consistency, and usability across growth touchpoints
  • A strong experimentation culture—leveraging A/B testing, data, and qualitative insights to continuously optimize experiences

Responsibilities

  • Lead and mentor a team of designers focused on growth and lifecycle experiences
  • Partner with Product and Engineering leadership to define strategy, priorities, and success metrics
  • Translate business goals into clear user experience strategies and scalable design solutions
  • Champion user-centered design while balancing speed, experimentation, and impact
  • Establish and refine processes for design discovery, iteration, and delivery
  • Use data, research, and experimentation to inform decisions and measure outcomes
  • Advocate for design at the executive level and contribute to broader product leadership discussions

What We’re Looking For

  • 8+ years of experience in product design , with 3+ years in a design leadership role
  • Proven experience designing B2B2C and/or SaaS products , ideally with complex workflows
  • Strong background in growth, onboarding, activation, or lifecycle design
  • Demonstrated ability to drive measurable outcomes (activation, retention, conversion, churn reduction)
  • Experience leading and scaling high-performing design teams
  • Strong product thinking and the ability to balance user needs with business goals
  • Comfort working in fast-paced, ambiguous environments with cross-functional partners
  • Excellent communication, storytelling, and stakeholder management skills

Nice to Have

  • Experience designing for agency platforms, CRM tools, or marketing technology
  • Familiarity with experimentation frameworks and growth metrics
  • Experience working in remote-first or distributed teams

Why GoHighLevel

  • Own some of the most impactful growth experiences in the product
  • Lead and shape a growing design organization
  • Work with a passionate, product-driven team solving meaningful problemsCompetitive compensation, benefits, and flexibility

EEO Statement

The company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. As an employer subject to affirmative action regulations, we invite you to voluntarily provide the following demographic information. This information is used solely for compliance with government recordkeeping, reporting, and other legal requirements. Providing this information is voluntary and refusal to do so will not affect your application status. This data will be kept separate from your application and will not be used in the hiring decision.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Head of Insurance - Business Solutions Group

The ideal candidate will drive pre-sales, architect complex deals for insurance clients, and lead a distributed team of solutions architects and pre sales professionals.Key ResponsibilitiesStrategic Leadership & Business DevelopmentPre-Sales Strategy: Define and execute pre-sales strategy aligned with Insurance business objectives, identifying growth opportunities in target segments (P&C, Life, Health, Specialty Insurance)Large Deal Leadership: Lead end-to-end pre-sales execution for enterprise-scale, complex insurance transformation initiatives globally, from opportunity assessment through proposal and solution architectureMarket Positioning: Develop compelling value propositions specific to insurance domain challenges (digital transformation, policy administration, claims automation, underwriting automation, data & analytics, fraud detection)Client Relationship Building: Build and strengthen relationships with insurance C-suite (CIOs, CTOs, Insurance Product Heads, Chief Digital Services Cloud, Guidewire Policy Center/Claims Center, INSR, EIS, etc.)Emerging Technologies: Hands-on or consulting experience with:Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning applications in insurance (claims prediction, fraud detection, customer segmentation)Data analytics and business intelligence for insuranceCloud-native architectures and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes)Sales & Business AcumenRFP/Proposal Excellence: Strong track record of winning large, competitive bids through compelling technical proposals and value articulationCommercial Awareness: Ability to understand customer business models, financial metrics, and ROI drivers; experience with deal structuring and pricingSales Enablement: Experience developing solution collateral, customer case studies, and sales tools that accelerate opportunity qualification and conversionNegotiation Skills: Proven ability to negotiate complex requirements, scope, and contractual terms with senior client stakeholdersEducationBE/B Tech, MBA
descriptionHtml:
Position Overview

We are seeking an exceptional Insurance Business Solutions Group (BSG) Head to lead the pre-sales, solution architecture, and offerings creation for our Insurance business globally. This is a high impact leadership role combining industry expertise, solution architecture excellence, and proven large-deal management capabilities. The ideal candidate will drive pre-sales, architect complex deals for insurance clients, and lead a distributed team of solutions architects and pre sales professionals.


Key Responsibilities


Strategic Leadership & Business Development

  • Pre-Sales Strategy: Define and execute pre-sales strategy aligned with Insurance business objectives, identifying growth opportunities in target segments (P&C, Life, Health, Specialty Insurance)
  • Large Deal Leadership: Lead end-to-end pre-sales execution for enterprise-scale, complex insurance transformation initiatives globally, from opportunity assessment through proposal and solution architecture
  • Market Positioning: Develop compelling value propositions specific to insurance domain challenges (digital transformation, policy administration, claims automation, underwriting automation, data & analytics, fraud detection)
  • Client Relationship Building: Build and strengthen relationships with insurance C-suite (CIOs, CTOs, Insurance Product Heads, Chief Digital Officers) and drive solution articulation


Solution Architecture & Technical Leadership

  • Complex Solution Design: Design end-to-end solutions for large scale insurance transformation programs, including policy administration systems, claims management, underwriting platforms, and data/analytics capabilities
  • RFP & Proposal Response: Lead technical proposal development for large deals, ensuring solutions are commercially competitive, technically sound, and aligned with customer objectives
  • Insurance Domain Expertise: Leverage deep knowledge of insurance business models, regulatory requirements, product lines, and operational challenges to design relevant, impactful solutions


Team Management & Enablement

  • Team Leadership: Build, develop, and lead a distributed Insurance BSG team including Solution Architects, Pre-Sales Consultants, and Business Analysts
  • Capability Building: Identify skill gaps and drive training/certification in insurance domain, solution architecture methodologies, and sales excellence
  • Performance Management: Set clear performance metrics, monitor KPIs (deal conversion rates, proposal quality, team productivity), and drive accountability
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with Sales, Delivery, Practices, and Ecosystem teams to ensure seamless transition from pre-sales to successful delivery


Required Qualifications


Experience

  • Minimum 15 years of professional experience in BFSI with at least 8-10 years in Insurance domain
  • Minimum 6-8 years in pre-sales, solution architecture, or enterprise consulting roles focused on insurance transformations
  • Proven track record managing large deals (minimum contract value $2-5M+) for enterprise insurance clients
  • Demonstrated expertise architecting complex, multi-module insurance solutions (policy administration, claims management, underwriting, billing, data & analytics)
  • Experience with global insurance operations and understanding of insurance business models across P&C, Life, Health, or Specialty insurance lines
  • Leadership experience managing pre-sales teams and mentoring solution architects


Domain & Technical Knowledge


Insurance Expertise: Deep understanding of:

  • Insurance products, underwriting, claims, policy administration, and billing processes
  • Insurance industry regulatory frameworks
  • Insurance-specific pain points and digital transformation imperatives
  • Major insurance players, their IT strategies, and typical IT spend patterns


Solution Architecture Competency:

  • Proficiency in designing enterprise solutions (design thinking, requirements analysis, trade-off analysis)
  • Cloud architecture knowledge (AWS, Azure, GCP) for insurance workloads
  • Understanding of insurance software platforms (major players: Salesforce Financial Services Cloud, Guidewire Policy Center/Claims Center, INSR, EIS, etc.)


Emerging Technologies: Hands-on or consulting experience with:

  • Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning applications in insurance (claims prediction, fraud detection, customer segmentation)
  • Data analytics and business intelligence for insurance
  • Cloud-native architectures and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes)


Sales & Business Acumen

  • RFP/Proposal Excellence: Strong track record of winning large, competitive bids through compelling technical proposals and value articulation
  • Commercial Awareness: Ability to understand customer business models, financial metrics, and ROI drivers; experience with deal structuring and pricing
  • Sales Enablement: Experience developing solution collateral, customer case studies, and sales tools that accelerate opportunity qualification and conversion
  • Negotiation Skills: Proven ability to negotiate complex requirements, scope, and contractual terms with senior client stakeholders


Education

  • BE/B Tech, MBA
