descriptionHtml:
About Us
QuillBot was founded in 2017 with a mission to help students and professionals strengthen their writing. Today, we help over 56 million people around the world create great things. Whether you're writing, designing, coding, or collaborating, QuillBot is a place where anyone can create at the speed of thought. Our AI-powered tools help you think clearly, communicate effectively, and create beautifully—across every platform, in any format, at any skill level. If you're passionate about using technology to make the path from inspiration to execution more accessible, intentional, and relevant, come join us.

Role Overview
We are looking for a strategic, high-ownership Director of Organic Growth to lead and scale QuillBot’s organic acquisition engine across all major discovery surfaces. This role goes well beyond traditional SEO—you will own the full spectrum of organic growth, including Search Engine Optimization (SEO), App Store Optimization (ASO), Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), and Extension Store visibility (Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, and others). As a Director, you will set the vision, build and manage the roadmap, and lead the existing SEO & Organic Growth team. You’ll partner closely with Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, and International teams to embed organic growth into QuillBot’s product and go-to-market strategy. Your work will directly influence organic traffic, sales, install volume, revenue, and brand visibility on a global scale.

Responsibilities

  • Strategy & Leadership
    • Organic Growth Strategy: Define and own the overarching organic growth strategy spanning SEO, ASO, GEO, and Extension Store optimization. Set quarterly and annual OKRs tied to impressions, clicks, MAU, installs, and sales. 
    • Team Leadership: Manage, mentor, and develop the SEO & Organic Growth team. Foster a culture of experimentation, accountability, and continuous learning. Identify and hire for key capability gaps as needed.
    • Roadmap Ownership: Build and maintain a prioritized organic growth roadmap. Balance quick wins with longer-term strategic bets. Present plans and progress to senior leadership, including the CEO.
    • Cross-Functional Partnership: Serve as the organic growth voice in cross-functional planning with AI, Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, Marketing Operations, and International Expansion teams. Ensure organic considerations are embedded in product launches, site architecture decisions, and content strategy.
    • Product Roadmap Influence: Act as a key influencer of the Product roadmap by surfacing organic growth opportunities, user intent data, and search landscape insights that shape feature prioritization, new product bets, and go-to-market timing.
  • Search Engine Optimization (SEO)
    • SEO Program Ownership: Own end-to-end SEO performance across QuillBot’s web properties. Drive strategy across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO to secure and defend top positions for high-value keywords.
    • Technical SEO: Lead technical audits and partner with Engineering to resolve crawlability, indexation, Core Web Vitals, site speed, structured data, and mobile-responsiveness issues at scale.
    • Content & Keyword Strategy: Oversee keyword research, content gap analysis, and on-page optimization. Collaborate with the Content team to ensure every page is strategically targeted and continuously improved.
    • Link Building & Authority: Direct link-building and digital PR strategies to grow domain authority. Evaluate backlink quality and build partnerships with authoritative sites.
    • Algorithm Resilience: Monitor Google algorithm updates, diagnose ranking fluctuations, and quickly adapt strategies to protect and grow organic traffic.
  • Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO)
    • GEO Strategy: Develop and execute a strategy to maximize QuillBot’s visibility in AI-generated answers and citations across generative search experiences (e.g., Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, etc).
    • Content for LLMs: Work with Content and Product to structure information, schema markup, and authoritative content so that generative engines accurately surface and recommend QuillBot.
    • Measurement & Experimentation: Define GEO-specific metrics, build tracking frameworks, and run experiments to understand how generative surfaces drive traffic and conversions.
    • Competitive Intelligence: Monitor how competitors are cited in generative answers and identify opportunities to increase QuillBot’s share of voice.
  • App Store Optimisation (ASO)
    • ASO Program Ownership: Own QuillBot’s organic discoverability and conversion rates in the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.
    • Listing Optimization: Optimize app titles, subtitles, descriptions, keywords, screenshots, and preview videos to improve search rankings and download conversion rates.
    • Ratings & Reviews: Develop strategies to increase the volume and quality of positive ratings and reviews, and work with Product to address recurring user feedback themes.
    • A/B Testing: Design and run store listing experiments to continuously improve conversion. Leverage tools like Google Play Experiments and third-party ASO platforms.
    • Market-Specific ASO: Partner with the International Expansion team to localize and optimize listings for priority markets.
  • Extension Store Optimisation
    • Extension Visibility: Own QuillBot’s presence and discoverability in the Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, Microsoft Edge Add-ons, and Firefox Add-ons.
    • Listing & CRO: Optimize extension titles, descriptions, screenshots, and feature graphics. Improve install conversion rates through iterative testing.
    • Ratings & Review Strategy: Drive rating volume and quality for extensions. Coordinate with Product and Support to address negative feedback and reduce churn.
    • Cross-Channel Synergy: Align extension store strategy with SEO and ASO efforts to create a unified organic discovery experience across web, app, and extension surfaces.
  • Analytics, Reporting & Insights
    • Performance Measurement: Build and maintain dashboards tracking organic performance across all channels (SEO, ASO, GEO, Extension Stores). Define KPIs and ensure the team is data-driven in decision-making.
    • Executive Reporting: Deliver regular performance updates and strategic insights to senior leadership. Translate complex organic data into clear narratives with actionable recommendations.
    • Tool Stack: Evaluate and manage the organic growth tool stack (e.g., Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Sensor Tower, Screaming Frog, etc).

Qualifications

  • 8–10 years of progressive experience in SEO and/or organic growth, with at least 3 years in a people-management role.
  • Global experience: demonstrated ability to drive organic growth across multiple markets, regions, and languages—not limited to English-speaking audiences.
  • Demonstrated success managing organic growth for a high-traffic website (+20M monthly active users) and a consumer app with significant install volume.
  • Proven track record of securing and defending #1 positions for competitive keywords
  • Proven ability to build organic visibility from zero for new product launches in highly competitive markets — taking a product from no search presence to top-ranking positions against established incumbents.
  • Deep expertise across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO, with a strong grasp of how search engines crawl, index, and rank content.
  • Hands-on experience with ASO for iOS and/or Android apps, including keyword optimization, listing experiments, and review management.
  • Strong understanding of the generative AI search landscape and emerging GEO best practices.
  • Proficiency with industry tools: Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, and at least one ASO platform (preferably Sensor Tower).
  • Experience working with CMS platforms (e.g., WordPress) and comfort with basic HTML/CSS.
  • Excellent communicator who can present data-driven strategies to senior leadership, including C-level stakeholders.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field.

Bonus Points

  • Experience at a SaaS, EdTech, or consumer-tech company with a freemium or subscription business model.
  • Hands-on experience with multilingual and multi-regional SEO and ASO strategies.
  • Familiarity with structured data, schema markup, and Knowledge Graph optimization.
  • Experience with programmatic SEO or large-scale page generation strategies.
  • Experience optimizing browser extension listings in the Chrome Web Store or equivalent platforms.
  • Background in conversion rate optimization (CRO) as it relates to organic landing pages and store listings.
  • Comfortable working across time zones in a fully remote, globally distributed team.

Why Join QuillBot?

  • Ownership & Impact: Take full ownership of organic growth across every discovery surface and directly influence QuillBot’s trajectory.
  • Scope & Challenge: Lead a function that spans SEO, ASO, GEO, and Extension Stores—a rare opportunity to shape organic strategy holistically.
  • Collaborative Culture: Work with a passionate, innovative team across Content, Marketing, Product, and Engineering.
  • Remote Flexibility: Enjoy the flexibility of working remotely with flexible hours to balance professional and personal life.
  • Professional Growth: QuillBot values learning and development, offering opportunities for skill enhancement and career advancement.

Benefits & Perks

  • Competitive salary and annual bonus
  • Medical coverage
  • Life and accidental insurance
  • Vacation & leaves of absence (menstrual, flexible, special, and more!)
  • Developmental opportunities through education & developmental reimbursements & professional workshops
  • Maternity & parental leave
  • Hybrid & remote model with flexible working hours
  • On-site & remote company events throughout the year
  • Tech & WFH stipends & new hire allowances
  • Employee referral program
  • Premium access to QuillBot

*Benefits and benefit amounts differ by region. A comprehensive list applicable to your region will be provided in your interview process. 

Research shows that candidates from underrepresented backgrounds often don't apply for roles if they don't meet all the criteria. We strongly encourage you to apply if you're interested: we'd love to learn how you can amplify our team with your unique experience!

This role is eligible for hire in India.

We are a virtual-first company with employees across the United States, Canada, India, Germany, and the Netherlands. Our compensation approach is market-based and varies by location while ensuring fairness, consistency, and transparency.

The base pay for this role is determined by several factors, including:

  • Relevant professional experience
  • Skills and expertise
  • Scope and responsibilities of the role
  • Internal equity considerations 

In addition to base salary, total compensation may include bonus, equity, and benefits. Eligibility depends on role, level, and location.

We are committed to equal pay for equal work, and all compensation decisions are made using consistent, gender-neutral criteria in alignment with pay transparency requirements

#QuillBot

Equal ​Employment Opportunity Statement ​(EEO)
We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity and ​inclusion within our company.  ​We will consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender ​identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, ​veteran status, or ​ability status. We will ensure that individuals who are differently abled are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment as provided to other applicants or employees. Please contact us to request accommodation.

title: Director, People Business Partner
description: Teamwork makes the stream work. Roku is changing how the world watches TVRoku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.  About the role Roku is seeking a Director, People Business Partner to lead strategic people initiatives for our Devices, Product & Technology organization from our growing Bengaluru office. This role is pivotal in shaping organizational effectiveness, driving change management, and building a high-performing team locally while partnering closely with senior leaders in our India, APAC, EMEA and our U.S. headquarters. As Roku enters its next phase of growth and profitability, you will lead local initiatives that strengthen our presence in India, foster a strong culture, and ensure alignment with global strategies. You will manage a small team and serve as a trusted advisor to senior leaders, influencing decisions that impact our global workforce.  Why join Roku in Bangalore? Our newly established Bengaluru office represents an exciting chapter in Roku’s global expansion. This is your opportunity to be part of building something from the ground up, shaping the culture, creating impactful programs, and influencing how we grow in one of the world’s most dynamic tech hubs. You’ll work in a collaborative environment that values innovation, transparency, and data-driven decision-making, while having a direct line to global leaders If you thrive on creating meaningful change, this is the role for you.  What You’ll Do Lead Local People Strategy: Drive initiatives that establish and grow Roku’s presence in Bangalore, ensuring compliance with local labor laws and cultural alignment. Strategic Partnership: Collaborate with senior leaders in globally to align People strategies with business priorities and influence decision-making at the leadership level. Team Leadership: Manage and develop People Business Partners, fostering collaboration and continuous improvement. Organizational Development: Diagnose organizational health, design interventions to improve effectiveness, and lead initiatives that strengthen leadership capability and team performance. Change Management: Develop and execute strategies to guide teams through organizational change, ensuring engagement and resilience across geographies. Talent & Leadership Development: Coach leaders to take ownership of talent development, succession planning, and performance management, modeling behaviors that reinforce Roku’s culture. Employee Experience: Partner with leaders to identify trends in engagement and performance and implement solutions that enhance the employee experience. Global Collaboration: Work across geographies to maintain a unified company culture while respecting local nuances. Data-Driven Insights: Use analytics and external benchmarks to inform strategies and measure impact, driving continuous improvement in People programs.  We’re excited if you have 15+ years of progressive HR/People Business Partner experience, including at least 5 years in a leadership role managing a team. Experience working in a global environment, with a US based headquarters. Proven success partnering with senior executives in high-growth, tech-driven environments, ideally supporting engineering and product organizations. Expertise in organizational development, change management, and leadership coaching. Strong ability to navigate ambiguity and introduce structure where needed. Experience leveraging data and metrics to inform decisions and measure organizational effectiveness. Exceptional communication and influencing skills, with the ability to engage stakeholders at all levels. A track record of building trust through transparency, integrity, and ethical decision-making. Deep knowledge of India’s labor laws and HR practices, with experience supporting global organizations.     Our Hybrid Work ApproachRoku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy. BenefitsRoku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Our employees can take time off work for vacation and other personal reasons to balance their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter. AccommodationsRoku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com. The Roku CultureRoku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV. We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002. To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet.By providing your information, you acknowledge that you want Roku to contact you about job roles, that you have read Roku's Applicant Privacy Notice, and understand that Roku will use your information as described in that notice. If you do not wish to receive any communications from Roku regarding this role or similar roles in the future, you may unsubscribe at any time by emailing WorkforcePrivacy@Roku.com.
descriptionHtml:
Teamwork makes the stream work.

 

Roku is changing how the world watches TV

Roku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.

From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.

 

About the role 


Roku is seeking a Director, People Business Partner to lead strategic people initiatives for our Devices, Product & Technology organization from our growing Bengaluru office. This role is pivotal in shaping organizational effectiveness, driving change management, and building a high-performing team locally while partnering closely with senior leaders in our India, APAC, EMEA and our U.S. headquarters. 

As Roku enters its next phase of growth and profitability, you will lead local initiatives that strengthen our presence in India, foster a strong culture, and ensure alignment with global strategies. You will manage a small team and serve as a trusted advisor to senior leaders, influencing decisions that impact our global workforce. 

 

Why join Roku in Bangalore? 

Our newly established Bengaluru office represents an exciting chapter in Roku’s global expansion. This is your opportunity to be part of building something from the ground up, shaping the culture, creating impactful programs, and influencing how we grow in one of the world’s most dynamic tech hubs. You’ll work in a collaborative environment that values innovation, transparency, and data-driven decision-making, while having a direct line to global leaders If you thrive on creating meaningful change, this is the role for you. 

 

What You’ll Do 
  • Lead Local People Strategy: Drive initiatives that establish and grow Roku’s presence in Bangalore, ensuring compliance with local labor laws and cultural alignment. 
  • Strategic Partnership: Collaborate with senior leaders in globally to align People strategies with business priorities and influence decision-making at the leadership level. 
  • Team Leadership: Manage and develop People Business Partners, fostering collaboration and continuous improvement. 
  • Organizational Development: Diagnose organizational health, design interventions to improve effectiveness, and lead initiatives that strengthen leadership capability and team performance. 
  • Change Management: Develop and execute strategies to guide teams through organizational change, ensuring engagement and resilience across geographies. 
  • Talent & Leadership Development: Coach leaders to take ownership of talent development, succession planning, and performance management, modeling behaviors that reinforce Roku’s culture. 
  • Employee Experience: Partner with leaders to identify trends in engagement and performance and implement solutions that enhance the employee experience. 
  • Global Collaboration: Work across geographies to maintain a unified company culture while respecting local nuances. 
  • Data-Driven Insights: Use analytics and external benchmarks to inform strategies and measure impact, driving continuous improvement in People programs. 

 

We’re excited if you have 
  • 15+ years of progressive HR/People Business Partner experience, including at least 5 years in a leadership role managing a team. 
  • Experience working in a global environment, with a US based headquarters. 
  • Proven success partnering with senior executives in high-growth, tech-driven environments, ideally supporting engineering and product organizations. 
  • Expertise in organizational development, change management, and leadership coaching. 
  • Strong ability to navigate ambiguity and introduce structure where needed. 
  • Experience leveraging data and metrics to inform decisions and measure organizational effectiveness. 
  • Exceptional communication and influencing skills, with the ability to engage stakeholders at all levels. 
  • A track record of building trust through transparency, integrity, and ethical decision-making. 
  • Deep knowledge of India’s labor laws and HR practices, with experience supporting global organizations. 

 

 

 

Our Hybrid Work Approach

Roku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy.

 

Benefits

Roku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Our employees can take time off work for vacation and other personal reasons to balance their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter.

 

Accommodations

Roku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com.

 

The Roku Culture

Roku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV. 

We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002. 

To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet.

By providing your information, you acknowledge that you want Roku to contact you about job roles, that you have read Roku's Applicant Privacy Notice, and understand that Roku will use your information as described in that notice. If you do not wish to receive any communications from Roku regarding this role or similar roles in the future, you may unsubscribe at any time by emailing WorkforcePrivacy@Roku.com.

descriptionHtml:
As Director, Software Engineering in our Advisor Platform Architecture & Engineering team, you'll lead a manager-of-managers organization to deliver a cutting-edge Advisor Platform. You'll set the technical roadmap and engineering standards, remove obstacles to team performance, and partner across Product, Design, and Operations. Anchored in our core values of Make it Happen, Serve, and One Team, you'll drive data-driven decisions that boost speed, quality, reliability, and cost efficiency—while fostering innovation, inclusion, and continuous learning.


What You’ll Do

  • Set and execute the engineering strategy for the Advisor Platform; translate business goals into a technical roadmap, operating mechanisms, and measurable outcomes (OKRs/KPIs).
  • Lead and develop a manager-of-managers organization—hire, coach, and retain diverse teams; build succession plans; cultivate an inclusive, high-performance culture.
  • Own delivery excellence across the SDLC; standardize Agile/DevOps practices (CI/CD, testing, observability, incident/postmortems) to improve reliability, security, and maintainability.
  • Make accountable, data-driven decisions on resourcing, budget, portfolio priorities, and sequencing; optimize total cost of ownership for platform components.
  • Establish and enforce engineering standards and controls (secure coding, data protection, change management, access governance) and carry governance accountability for quality, security, and maintainability.
  • Influence peer leaders and senior stakeholders across Product, Design, Data, Operations, Security, and Compliance; provide clear narratives and metrics to guide trade-offs and decisions.
  • Maintain deep domain awareness of wealth management regulations and client sensitivities (e.g., data privacy, suitability, fiduciary obligations); partner with Compliance to keep standards current.
  • Proactively identify and mitigate risk; lead incident response and root cause remediation; ensure all solutions adhere to internal standards and applicable regulatory guidelines.
  • Drive architecture and modernization initiatives (cloud/platform evolution, build vs. buy, legacy remediation) that increase scalability, resilience, and speed of delivery.


What You Bring

  • Engineering leadership at scale: Proven track record leading manager-of-managers organizations and developing high-performing teams (hiring, coaching, succession, inclusion).
  • Modern SDLC & delivery excellence: Deep familiarity with Agile, DevOps, CI/CD, testing/automation, observability, incident response/postmortems, and reliability practices.
  • Architecture & cloud: Proven experience designing and delivering large-scale, cloud-native platforms (AWS/Azure/GCP) with strong governance (security, reliability, observability) and cost-of-ownership optimization.
  • Security & risk: Strong knowledge of secure coding, compliance, and risk management within technology organizations; able to embed controls throughout the SDLC.
  • Stakeholder influence: Demonstrated ability to collaborate across matrixed environments and influence senior stakeholders using clear narratives and data.
  • Operational judgment: Strong analytical/problem-solving skills with hands-on experience in incident management and issue resolution; comfort making data-driven trade-offs across scope, schedule, and quality.


Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).
  • 17+ years of significant, progressively responsible experience in software engineering including 5+ years leading managers/technologists to anticipate and solve complex technical and operational challenges. (Equivalent experience considered.)
  • Demonstrated delivery of large-scale, cloud-native solutions and ownership of SDLC excellence (Agile/DevOps/CI/CD, reliability).
  • Financial services/regulatory literacy—deep understanding of regulatory requirements and client sensitivities (e.g., data privacy, suitability, auditability) in regulated environments.


Preferred Qualifications

  • Master’s degree in CS/Engineering (or related) or an MBA with strong technical depth.
  • Direct Wealth Management platform experience.
  • Prior track record modernizing legacy platforms and accelerating cloud migrations.
  • Experience with multi-region architectures, SOCs/SOX audit readiness, or PCI/SOC 2 implementations.

descriptionHtml:
Role Overview


The Director – Product & Cloud Security owns the strategy, architecture, and execution of security across the product lifecycle and cloud platforms, ensuring that Odessa’s SaaS products are secure by design, compliant by default, and resilient at scale.


This role leads Product Security, Cloud Security, and AI Security integration efforts, working closely with Engineering, Architecture, Product Management, DevOps/SRE, Compliance, and Legal teams. The Director is accountable for embedding security controls into modern cloud‑native architectures while supporting regulatory compliance, customer assurance, and business growth.


What You'll Do:


1. Product Security Leadership

  • Define and execute the Product Security strategy across design, development, testing, release, and operations.
  • Embed security into SDLC and CI/CD pipelines, ensuring consistent adoption across engineering teams.
  • Lead threat modeling, secure architecture reviews, and design risk assessments for new and existing product capabilities.
  • Govern application security testing programs, including:
  • SAST, DAST, and SCA
  • Infrastructure‑as‑Code (IaC) and API security testing
  • Own product vulnerability management, including prioritization, remediation SLAs, and customer‑facing disclosures.
  • Partner with Product and Engineering leadership to ensure security supports speed, scale, and innovation.


2. Cloud & Platform Security

  • Own security architecture and posture for cloud‑native SaaS platforms hosted on Microsoft Azure.
  • Define and enforce cloud security guardrails across:
  • Identity & access management (IAM, MFA, PAM)
  • Network security, WAFs, and segmentation
  • Encryption, key management, logging, and monitoring
  • Lead Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) and continuous configuration assurance.
  • Oversee security for containers, Kubernetes, APIs, and microservices.
  • Ensure effective integration and use of core security platforms, including:
  • Azure Security Center / Defender
  • FortiGate Firewall & FortiWeb WAF
  • CyberArk (PAM)
  • Lacework (CSPM)
  • Sumo Logic (SIEM)
  • Datadog (APM & monitoring)
  • CrowdStrike (endpoint & workload protection)
  • Tenable (vulnerability management)


3. AI Security & Secure AI Integration (New – Core Accountability)

  • Define and own the AI Security strategy for product features leveraging AI/ML and GenAI capabilities.
  • Ensure AI features are designed and deployed in line with Responsible AI, privacy, and security‑by‑design principles.
  • Partner with Product and Engineering teams to:
  • Perform AI threat modeling (model abuse, prompt injection, data leakage, training data risks).
  • Establish guardrails for AI usage, including data classification, access controls, and output validation.
  • Govern security controls for:
  • Training data protection and lineage
  • Model integrity and lifecycle management
  • Secure integration of third‑party AI services and APIs
  • Align AI security practices with:
  • Internal AI Security and Responsible Use policies
  • Emerging regulations and frameworks (e.g., EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, data protection laws)
  • Act as a key member of the AI Governance Committee, providing security and risk leadership.


4. Governance, Risk & Compliance (Product & Cloud Scope)

  • Ensure product and cloud controls meet ISO 27001/27017, SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, DPDPA, and customer contractual requirements.
  • Support customer security reviews, RFPs, audits, and due‑diligence activities.
  • Translate regulatory and contractual obligations into practical, auditable technical controls.
  • Maintain strong documentation, evidence, and metrics for audits and certifications.


5. Incident Response & Security Operations

  • Act as a senior escalation point for product and cloud security incidents.
  • Ensure effective detection, response, root‑cause analysis, and post‑incident improvements.
  • Partner with SOC and engineering teams to improve mean‑time‑to‑detect and remediate.


6. Leadership & Team Management

  • Build, lead, and mentor Product Security and Cloud Security teams.
  • Define team charters, KPIs, and maturity roadmaps.
  • Drive a culture where security is a shared responsibility, not a gatekeeper.
  • Represent Product & Cloud Security in executive, customer, and board‑level discussions.


Basic Qualifications :


Required

  • 12+ years in product security, application security, or cloud security, with 5+ years in leadership roles.
  • Strong hands‑on and architectural experience with Azure‑based SaaS platforms.
  • Deep understanding of secure SDLC, CI/CD security, cloud‑native architectures, and modern DevSecOps practices.
  • Proven experience operating tools such as SAST/DAST/SCA, CSPM, SIEM, PAM, WAFs, and vulnerability management platforms.
  • Experience supporting SOC 2 / ISO 27001 audits and enterprise customer security assessments.
  • Ability to communicate security risk effectively to engineering leaders, executives, auditors, and customers.


Preferred Qualifications


  • Experience in FinTech, financial services, or highly regulated SaaS environments.
  • Exposure to AI/ML security, GenAI risk management, and AI governance.

descriptionHtml:
Job Title: Director AI & Data Analytics

Budget: Upto 1.1 Cr (Fixed + Variable, based on experience and fit)


Role Summary:

The Director of Data Science & AI will lead efforts in building and scaling AI-driven products and services in this company. This role requires an AI leader who has built AI and data science teams from the ground up, managed teams ranging from 10 to 100+, and successfully delivered AI powered products. This leader will be responsible for working with the leadership team across functions to define AI strategy, managing geographically distributed AI and data science teams across the US and Bangalore, and ensuring AI innovation aligns with business objectives and regulatory requirements in the healthcare funding and payments industry.


Key Responsibilities:


AI & Data Science Strategy & Execution:


• Collaborate with cross-functional leadership (Product, Engineering, Operations, Compliance, and Finance) to define and drive Alegeus’ AI and data science strategy.

• Lead the development of agentic AI products that automate decision-making, enhance personalization, and improve operational efficiencies.

• Identify high-impact AI and machine learning use cases, including predictive analytics, fraud detection, automated customer service, and intelligent financial management tools.

• Ensure AI initiatives are ethically designed, regulatory-compliant (HIPAA, GDPR, etc.), and secure in handling sensitive healthcare and financial data.

• Manage Data Engineering teams delivering on a vision of an AI-ready enterprise data platform, ensuring seamless data integration, transformation, and optimization to support advanced AI capabilities.


Team Leadership & Organizational AI Maturity:


• Build and scale an AI and data science practice from the ground up, hiring, managing, and mentoring a high-performing team of Data Engineers, AI Engineers, Data Scientists, and ML Engineers.

• Lead teams from 10 to 100 members, ensuring talent acquisition, training, and retention strategies foster a world-class AI organization.

• Manage geographically distributed teams across the US and Bangalore, ensuring seamless collaboration, cultural alignment, and operational efficiency.

• Drive AI literacy across the organization by collaborating with product, engineering, and business teams to integrate AI capabilities into Alegeus' core offerings.

• Cultivate a culture of AI-first thinking, innovation, and cross-functional collaboration.


AI Product Development & Deployment:


• Lead the design, development, and deployment of AI-powered products that create tangible business value in healthcare payments, funding accounts, and personalized consumer experiences. • Oversee AI-powered conversational agents, workflow automation, and decision-support systems to improve customer and partner interactions.

• Work closely with product and engineering teams to deploy AI solutions at scale within Alegeus’ technology ecosystem.

• Implement MLOps best practices to ensure AI models are robust, continuously improving, and operationally efficient.


Data Strategy & Governance:


• Work with the enterprise AI taskforce and data governance frameworks that ensure compliance, security, and responsible AI practices.

• Drive data strategy to optimize data pipelines, analytics infrastructure, and AI model performance.

• Leverage AI to enhance data-driven decision-making, risk assessment, and fraud prevention across healthcare financial transactions.


Industry Leadership & Innovation:


• Keep Alegeus at the forefront of AI-driven healthcare payment solutions, identifying and integrating emerging AI trends and technologies.

• Build external partnerships with AI research institutions, healthcare providers, and fintech innovators to accelerate AI-driven capabilities.

• Represent Alegeus in industry conferences, AI thought leadership forums, and regulatory discussions on AI in healthcare finance.


Qualifications & Experience Must-Have Qualifications:


• 15+ years of experience in Analytics, Data Science, AI, or closely related fields, with at least 5 years in a leadership role (Director or above).

• Proven experience building AI and data science teams from scratch and scaling teams from 10 to 100.

• Successfully delivered AI-powered products, from conception to deployment, that created real business impact.

• Deep knowledge of AI/ML technologies, LLMs, NLP, computer vision, and reinforcement learning.

• 7+ years in Software Product Company delivering products to market.

• Strong understanding of AI governance, ethics, data privacy laws (HIPAA, GDPR, etc.), and regulatory compliance in AI-driven healthcare payment systems.

• Experience in MLOps, cloud AI deployment (AWS, Azure, GCP), and data infrastructure optimization.

• Proven ability to manage geographically distributed teams across the US and Bangalore, ensuring productivity, cultural alignment, and operational efficiency.

• Experience managing Data Engineering teams to support an AI-ready enterprise data platform.

• Demonstrated ability to work across leadership functions to integrate AI strategy into business goals.

• Excellent leadership, strategic thinking, and cross-functional collaboration skills.


Preferred Qualifications:


• Prior experience leading AI innovation in a healthcare payments or fintech environment.

• Background in financial modeling, AI-powered payment systems, or healthcare funding analytics.

• Experience with AI-powered consumer engagement tools, AI-driven claims processing, or smart financial assistants.

• Strong relationships with AI research labs, tech vendors, and industry leaders.


Why Join this Company?


• Industry Leadership: Be at the forefront of AI-driven transformation in healthcare funding and payments.

• High-Impact Role: Shape the future of AI in healthcare finance and influence how millions manage their healthcare expenses.

• Growth Opportunity: Lead a team from early-stage AI adoption to a mature AI-driven organization.

• Global Exposure: Work with and manage distributed teams across the US and Bangalore, driving AI innovation at scale.

• Innovative Culture: Work in a fast-paced environment with a leadership team committed to AI and data-driven decision-making.

title: Regional Manager- Expansion
sector: Transportation, Logistics, Supply Chain and Storage and Internet Marketplace Platforms
description: About the CompanyFounded in 2015, Rapido is India’s leading multi-modal mobility platform. What began with bike taxis has rapidly evolved to include auto-rickshaws, cab-hailing, and peer-to-peer deliveries. Today, Rapido commands a dominant 70% market share in bike taxis, 40% in auto rides, and holds the second-largest position in the cab segment with a 22% share—establishing us as the overall market leader in ride-hailing. With over 4 million rides completed daily and more than 2 billion rides served to date, Rapido operates in 200+ cities and is aggressively expanding toward a presence in 500 cities. In 2024, we proudly achieved unicorn status with a valuation of $1.1 billion. Driven by technology and innovation, we’re expanding into new categories while remaining committed to improving last-mile connectivity and empowering livelihoods at scale. We are not an option; we are a choice!About the RoleAs a Regional Manager you are responsible for growing and maintaining reliable service delivery of your cities. You are the micro-CEO for your markets and are involved in building both the supply as well as the demand sides of the market with a strong focus on ensuring order fulfillment and a good experience for both the customer and the riders. You should be excited to identify key marketplace problems and collaborate effectively with supply, product, marketing and other supporting functions to drive growth in your cities. You are expected to have a balance between tactics and long-term sustainable initiatives and have good leadership skills so as to keep your team motivated, guide them along and deliver results.ResponsibilitiesOwning and delivering the aggressive revenue [P&L] and ride [Growth] targetsDevelop in-depth product and market understanding to identify the right target segments.Assess business requirements and recommend product improvements to help increase market penetration.Build, nurture and grow a vibrant team - provide guidance and mentorship to develop your team members into future leaders.Improvise customer pricing, discounting strategy, partner incentivization using sound analytical ideas and smart market [competitor] assessment.Conduct periodic review of the team performance, metrics and trends to identify and implement appropriate interventions in line with Rapido values, processes and business practices. descriptionHtml:
About the Company



Founded in 2015, Rapido is India’s leading multi-modal mobility platform. What began with bike taxis has rapidly evolved to include auto-rickshaws, cab-hailing, and peer-to-peer deliveries. Today, Rapido commands a dominant 70% market share in bike taxis, 40% in auto rides, and holds the second-largest position in the cab segment with a 22% share—establishing us as the overall market leader in ride-hailing. With over 4 million rides completed daily and more than 2 billion rides served to date, Rapido operates in 200+ cities and is aggressively expanding toward a presence in 500 cities. In 2024, we proudly achieved unicorn status with a valuation of $1.1 billion. Driven by technology and innovation, we’re expanding into new categories while remaining committed to improving last-mile connectivity and empowering livelihoods at scale. We are not an option; we are a choice!



About the Role



As a Regional Manager you are responsible for growing and maintaining reliable service delivery of your cities. You are the micro-CEO for your markets and are involved in building both the supply as well as the demand sides of the market with a strong focus on ensuring order fulfillment and a good experience for both the customer and the riders. You should be excited to identify key marketplace problems and collaborate effectively with supply, product, marketing and other supporting functions to drive growth in your cities. You are expected to have a balance between tactics and long-term sustainable initiatives and have good leadership skills so as to keep your team motivated, guide them along and deliver results.



Responsibilities



  • Owning and delivering the aggressive revenue [P&L] and ride [Growth] targets
  • Develop in-depth product and market understanding to identify the right target segments.
  • Assess business requirements and recommend product improvements to help increase market penetration.
  • Build, nurture and grow a vibrant team - provide guidance and mentorship to develop your team members into future leaders.
  • Improvise customer pricing, discounting strategy, partner incentivization using sound analytical ideas and smart market [competitor] assessment.
  • Conduct periodic review of the team performance, metrics and trends to identify and implement appropriate interventions in line with Rapido values, processes and business practices.


Collaboration



  • Work closely with the technical and product team to solve bugs or introduce new features which could improve a seamless driver partner experience.
  • Work closely with the central Operations team to solve for geographical or product level changes in the product/processes to improve customer experience.


Qualifications



  • 4 - 8 years overall experience
  • MBA (Tier-1) with 2-3 years of Operations / relevant experience preferred.


Required Skills



  • Excellent project and people management skills
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
  • Analytical bent of mind and data backed decision making skills.
  • Ability to work under pressure with limited resources and tight timelines.
  • Passionate, result-oriented with strong bias for action

descriptionHtml:
Every day, tens of millions of people come to Roblox to explore, create, play, learn, and connect with friends in 3D immersive digital experiences– all created by our global community of developers and creators.

At Roblox, we’re building the tools and platform that empower our community to bring any experience that they can imagine to life. Our vision is to reimagine the way people come together, from anywhere in the world, and on any device. We’re on a mission to connect a billion people with optimism and civility, and looking for amazing talent to help us get there.

A career at Roblox means you’ll be working to shape the future of human interaction, solving unique technical challenges at scale, and helping to create safer, more civil shared experiences for everyone.

WHY ROBLOX?

Roblox is ushering in the next generation of entertainment, allowing people to imagine, create, and play together in immersive, user-generated worlds. We’re the one and only fastest-growing entertainment platform that lets anyone teach themselves how to code, publish, and monetize any experience imaginable—across any device—reaching millions of players across the globe.

As a Public Policy Director for India & South Asia, you will be playing a key role in our engagement with Indian policymakers. The role will bolster our local strategy, including our participation in trade groups, work with NGOs, regulatory commitments and compliance, and proactive work to scale our business to best position Roblox for the future. You will be responsible for understanding our Community Rules and other policies and helping to develop and refine specific principles and policies. The ideal candidate will be able to work independently and remotely from HQ, as well as collaboratively with all internal stakeholders. You’re comfortable identifying policy issues, solving those issues, and bringing improved products to our players and creators. You believe in the value of in person, high touch meetings with policymakers.

You should be passionate about our mission, the gaming ecosystem, the community we serve, and the policy issues we need to address. Above all else, you appreciate that relationships often drive outcomes and work excellently and collaboratively with others.

You Will

  • Contribute to our comprehensive Public Policy strategy for India & South Asia and implement related programs
  • Identify, monitor, and analyze policy issues affecting Roblox in India & South Asia
  • Advise Roblox internal stakeholders on public policy matters as inputs to the development of our products and business operations
  • Articulate our public policy positions in briefing papers and consultation responses
  • Communicate with external stakeholders on behalf of Roblox on public policy topics
  • Communicate the public policies to all internal stakeholders at the company. Provide governance for and stakeholder updates on product policies
  • Ensure that Roblox stays in compliance with our product policies
  • Oversee and influence change necessary to achieve policy compliance objectives and foster a culture of compliance across the organization.
  • Work with external stakeholders, to understand and account for the views of external stakeholders about our product policies

You Have

  • A Bachelor's degree in Policy, Law, Media, Science, Engineering, Technology, or related field of study or equivalent.
  • 10-15 years of experience as a Public Policy Leader at an internet, e-commerce, social, or gaming company
  • Experience working with policymakers, child safety groups and international organizations
  • Seamlessly with cross-functional teams consisting of business operations, financial and legal stakeholders
  • Experience with project/program management and able to keep multiple concurrent deliverables moving forward on schedule

You Are

  • Passionate about public policy issues, domestic and international
  • A dynamic and engaging presenter with stellar written and oral delivery across multiple platforms, live and virtual
  • Willing to be on the road when necessary for outreach meetings and programs
  • An excellent written and oral communicator
  • Detail-oriented, with strong communication, planning and organizational skills
  • Collaborative, with strong relationship building skills to get things done in partnership with other groups
  • Self-directed, with the ability to define your own work and processes
  • Low drama, and treat everyone like a client regardless of level

Roles that are based in an office are onsite Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday, with optional presence on Monday and Friday (unless otherwise noted).

Roblox provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws. Roblox also provides reasonable accommodations to candidates with qualifying disabilities or religious beliefs during the recruiting process.

For US based roles only, please note the Company may not be able to employ candidates for this role who have United States work authorization related to certain U.S. visa categories, or support future H-1B sponsorship at this time.

descriptionHtml:
About CitiusTech:

CitiusTech is a global IT services, consulting, and business solutions enterprise 100% focused on the healthcare and life sciences industry. We enable 140+ enterprises to build a human-first ecosystem that is efficient, effective, and equitable with deep domain expertise and next-gen technology. With over 8,500 healthcare technology professionals worldwide, CitiusTech powers healthcare digital innovation, business transformation, and industry-wide convergence through next-generation technologies, solutions, and products.


Role Overview:

Job Description - Engineering Director for Implementation


Key Responsibilities:

  • Ownership of product implementations and client success, design and develop client specific customizations, manage timelines & risk, own P&L for the implementation team, drive collaboration with product management, engineering, and account management. Mahalingam is scheduled for this position.
  • 10+ years experience in building high performance agile engineering teams, with demonstrable expertise in managing scope, timelines and quality
  • Should have led a team of 40+ engineers in product engineering setups
  • Should be a PMP/CSM certified, TOGAF or equivalent certification is good to have
  • Should be hands-on in data engineering on technologies like Databricks, Unity catalog, DLT, Pyspark, One Lake, Sql server, No Sql
  • Should have good understanding of Micro services architecture using technologies like Java, Angular, Node.js
  • Should have good understanding of Cloud services and security patterns on Azure or AWS eg. Data Fabric, Azure Data Factory (ADF), One Lake, AWS Glue etc
  • Should have good understanding of Devops, DevSecops, Release management processes, Cloud architecture patterns on Azure or AW
  • Should have good understanding of AI and Gen AI technologies like Python, Machine learning, Langchain, Azure AI Foundry, A2A protocol, MCP protocol etc
  • Excellent understanding of Product life cycle and building scalable multi-tenant platforms and products in healthcare or life sciences domain
  • Excellent Stakeholder management and communication skills


Life at CitiusTech:

We focus on building highly motivated engineering teams and thought leaders with an entrepreneurial mindset, centered on our core values of Passion, Respect, Openness, Unity, and Depth (PROUD) of knowledge. Our success lies in creating a fun, transparent, non-hierarchical, diverse work culture that focuses on continuous learning and work-life balance.

Rated by our employees as the ‘Great Place to Work for’ according to the Great Place to Work survey. We offer you a comprehensive set of benefits to ensure that you have a long and rewarding career with us.


Our EVP:

Be You Be Awesome is our EVP and it reflects our continuing efforts to create CitiusTech as a great place to work where our employees can thrive, both personally and professionally. It encompasses the unique benefits and opportunities we offer to support your growth, well-being, and success throughout your journey with us and beyond. Together with our clients, we are solving some of the greatest healthcare challenges and positively impacting human lives. Welcome to the world of Faster Growth, Higher Learning, and Stronger Impact.

descriptionHtml:
Job Title: Senior Director-Product & Information Security

Location: Bangalore

Job Type: Full Time, Hybrid

Immediate joiners or notice period less than 15days are needed


This Role:

The Senior Director - Product & Information Security at LogixHealth will lead the efforts to ensure the organization and our partners are as secure as possible. This role will help ensure that security is always front and center across all our products, systems, platforms and tools within our ever growing environment. This role will work with a globally distributed team of engineers working in our fast-paced environment.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Own and lead end-to-end Product/Application Security, Cloud & Data Center Security, and IT Security for a US HealthTech organization
  • Provide strategic and operational leadership for the Security Operations Center (SOC), including detection, monitoring, incident response, escalation, and reporting
  • Establish and govern secure SDLC practices across all applications, including threat modeling, SAST, DAST, dependency scanning, penetration testing, and remediation tracking
  • Own security architecture and controls across cloud platforms, hybrid infrastructure, and on-prem data centers, ensuring strong identity, network, and data protection
  • Partner closely with Product, Engineering, IT, and Platform teams to embed security-by-design without slowing product delivery or impacting patient/user experience
  • Define, implement, and enforce information security governance, policies, standards, and procedures aligned with healthcare regulatory requirements
  • Ensure continuous compliance and audit readiness for HIPAA, SOC 2, HITRUST (preferred), ISO 27001, and other applicable US healthcare regulations
  • Lead incident response and crisis management, including tabletop exercises, breach simulations, regulatory notifications, and post-incident reviews
  • Oversee vulnerability management, risk assessments, security testing, and remediation across applications, infrastructure, endpoints, and third-party vendors
  • Drive security automation across SOC operations, compliance reporting, control monitoring, and alerting to improve speed, accuracy, and scale
  • Build and maintain strong risk management and reporting frameworks, providing clear visibility into security posture, key risks, and mitigation progress to executive leadership
  • Foster a strong security-first culture through ongoing awareness programs, phishing simulations, workshops, and targeted training for technical and non-technical teams
  • Lead, mentor, and scale a high-performing security, compliance, and SOC team, ensuring clear accountability, skill development, and succession planning
  • Manage relationships with security vendors, MSSPs, auditors, regulators, and external partners, ensuring SLA adherence and cost-effective outcomes
  • Act as a trusted advisor to executive leadership on security risks, regulatory exposure, and business trade-offs in a healthcare environment


Qualifications:

The following requirements are representative of the knowledge, skills, and/or ability required to perform this job successfully. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the duties.


Required:

  • 12+ years of progressive experience across Product/Application Security, Cloud/Infrastructure Security, and SOC operations, with at least 3+ years in a senior leadership capacity
  • Demonstrated experience owning or leading a SOC function, including incident response, threat detection, security monitoring, and operational governance
  • Proven ability to define and execute cybersecurity strategy, roadmap, and long-term security vision
  • Strong balance of strategic leadership and hands-on execution, with the ability to drive both direction and delivery
  • Proven expertise in Application Security, including Penetration Testing, Secure SDLC, DevSecOps, vulnerability management, and secure architecture principles
  • Deep expertise in cloud security controls across AWS, Azure, and GCP, including container and Kubernetes security
  • Strong experience in security governance, compliance, and risk management, including ISO 27001 / ISO 2700x, SOC 2, GDPR, HIPAA, HITRUST, and audit readiness
  • Demonstrated experience embedding security controls and best practices into engineering workflows and driving adoption across development teams
  • Proven ability to work cross-functionally with Engineering, DevOps, Legal, Compliance, Risk, and business stakeholders
  • Strong ability to communicate and influence executives, auditors, regulators, and cross-functional leadership teams
  • Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and people leadership skills, with a strong bias toward ownership, accountability, and measurable outcomes

title: Vice President - Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance)
sector: Financial Services, IT Services and IT Consulting, and Technology, Information and Internet
description: Our PurposeMastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.Title And SummaryVice President - Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance)Vice President, Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance)Who is Mastercard?Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships, and innovation, we help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential. Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture of inclusion and respect. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all.OverviewAs Vice President, Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance), you will lead Mastercard’s enterprise-wide value quantification program – scaling and unifying our current Services Value Quantification (SVQ) into a holistic Mastercard Value Quantification (MVQ) capability across Core, Commercial & New Payment Flows (CNPF), and Services. Reporting to the Senior Vice President, Value Enablement & Optimization, you will own the program’s strategy, governance, and execution. Your mission is to embed a value-first culture and ensure that quantifying customer value is at the heart of how Mastercard designs, sells, and delivers its solutions. You will establish a scalable, persona-based MVQ framework and platform to provide tailored value narratives and ROI insights. This role serves as a critical enterprise connector – collaborating across Product, Sales, Pricing, Customer Success, Finance, Technology, and regional teams – to drive broad adoption and tangible impact of MVQ globally. (This position reports to the Senior Vice President, Value Enablement & Optimization, within Mastercard’s Services organization.)Key Responsibilities Enterprise MVQ Vision & Leadership: Define and champion the enterprise MVQ vision and roadmap. Lead the evolution of SVQ into one unified MVQ program that quantifies and communicates the value of Mastercard’s solutions across all lines of business. Program Management & Governance: Establish and chair an MVQ governance framework that aligns cross-functional priorities, standardizes methodologies, and eliminates duplicative value quantification efforts. Implement a disciplined operating model with clear priorities and accountability. Holistic Value Story & Scalable Platform: Develop Mastercard’s cohesive value story with persona-specific value propositions and ROI narratives for key audiences (e.g., account teams, product teams, pricing, executives). Oversee MVQ platform enhancements to deliver these insights at scale, enabling users to generate tailored, customer-ready value analyses. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams – including Product, Sales (Account Management Customer Solutions Center, Services Business Leads), Pricing, Customer Success, and others – to embed MVQ into product, pricing, sales, and customer engagement processes, driving a unified, value-focused approach across the customer lifecycle. Adoption & Enablement: Drive enterprise adoption of MVQ and practices. Develop training, communications, and support programs to integrate MVQ into the daily workflows of client-facing teams. Use feedback and metrics to continually improve the platform and value delivery. Platform & Data Stewardship: Guide the ongoing development of the MVQ platform, working with Technology and Analytics partners to integrate new data sources, product value models, and analytical capabilities. Ensure the platform is robust, intuitive, and compliant with Mastercard’s standards. People Leadership: Build and lead a high-performing MVQ team. Provide mentorship, coaching, and clear direction to develop talent and expertise. Foster a collaborative, innovative, and results-driven culture that embraces a value-first mindset and grows the next generation of value experts.Key Skills & Qualifications Extensive experience: 15+ years in strategy, program management, consulting, or related roles leading large-scale, cross-functional initiatives in complex organizations (payments, financial services, or B2B technology sector preferred). Strategic & analytical acumen: Proven strength in strategic thinking, financial analysis, and data-driven problem solving. Adept at developing ROI models and translating analytics into actionable business insights. Value & commercial expertise: Familiarity with value-based selling, pricing strategy, and customer value management. Ability to articulate and quantify the business value of technology or service solutions to customers and internal stakeholders. Leadership & influence: Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and influence senior executives and diverse stakeholders. Skilled at driving alignment in a matrixed environment through vision and collaboration. Change management: Demonstrated success in driving adoption of new tools, processes, or cultural initiatives at scale. Passion for evangelizing innovation, with a track record of leading organizational change and embedding new practices. People development: Strong people management capabilities. Experience building and mentoring high-performing teams, developing talent, and fostering an inclusive, high-energy culture.Leadership Attributes Visionary & enterprise-minded: Strategic thinker who sees the big picture and can articulate a compelling, forward-looking vision for value quantification as a competitive differentiator. Collaborative influencer: Excels at leading through influence and building consensus. Cultivates trust and teamwork across functions, geographies, and levels to achieve shared goals. Results-focused & accountable: Driven to deliver tangible results. Sets high standards, measures progress and takes ownership to ensure execution and business impact. Innovative & adaptable: Embraces change with a continuous improvement mindset. Stays ahead of industry trends (e.g., data analytics, AI) to evolve capabilities and encourage creative solutions. Authentic & inclusive leader: Exemplifies Mastercard’s values and Decency Quotient (DQ). Creates a culture of integrity, inclusion, and empowerment. Inspires and develops others through mentorship and leading by example.Success Measures Widespread Adoption: MVQ is widely utilized across Mastercard’s businesses, with strong engagement from product, sales, account management, and customer success teams globally. Measurable Impact: MVQ drives tangible commercial outcomes – influencing key deal wins, supporting value-based pricing and renewals, and helping demonstrate ROI to customers – resulting in improved client satisfaction, retention, and revenue growth. Executive Alignment: MVQ is championed by senior leadership as a critical enterprise capability. Its insights are regularly incorporated into strategic planning, business reviews, and go-to-market decisions, reinforcing Mastercard’s value-driven culture and competitive differentiation.Corporate Security Responsibility All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;Report any suspected information security violation or breach, andComplete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.
descriptionHtml:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title And Summary

Vice President - Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance)

Vice President, Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance)

Who is Mastercard?

Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships, and innovation, we help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential. Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture of inclusion and respect. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all.

Overview

As Vice President, Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance), you will lead Mastercard’s enterprise-wide value quantification program – scaling and unifying our current Services Value Quantification (SVQ) into a holistic Mastercard Value Quantification (MVQ) capability across Core, Commercial & New Payment Flows (CNPF), and Services. Reporting to the Senior Vice President, Value Enablement & Optimization, you will own the program’s strategy, governance, and execution. Your mission is to embed a value-first culture and ensure that quantifying customer value is at the heart of how Mastercard designs, sells, and delivers its solutions. You will establish a scalable, persona-based MVQ framework and platform to provide tailored value narratives and ROI insights. This role serves as a critical enterprise connector – collaborating across Product, Sales, Pricing, Customer Success, Finance, Technology, and regional teams – to drive broad adoption and tangible impact of MVQ globally. (This position reports to the Senior Vice President, Value Enablement & Optimization, within Mastercard’s Services organization.)

Key Responsibilities

  • Enterprise MVQ Vision & Leadership: Define and champion the enterprise MVQ vision and roadmap. Lead the evolution of SVQ into one unified MVQ program that quantifies and communicates the value of Mastercard’s solutions across all lines of business.
  • Program Management & Governance: Establish and chair an MVQ governance framework that aligns cross-functional priorities, standardizes methodologies, and eliminates duplicative value quantification efforts. Implement a disciplined operating model with clear priorities and accountability.
  • Holistic Value Story & Scalable Platform: Develop Mastercard’s cohesive value story with persona-specific value propositions and ROI narratives for key audiences (e.g., account teams, product teams, pricing, executives). Oversee MVQ platform enhancements to deliver these insights at scale, enabling users to generate tailored, customer-ready value analyses.
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams – including Product, Sales (Account Management Customer Solutions Center, Services Business Leads), Pricing, Customer Success, and others – to embed MVQ into product, pricing, sales, and customer engagement processes, driving a unified, value-focused approach across the customer lifecycle.
  • Adoption & Enablement: Drive enterprise adoption of MVQ and practices. Develop training, communications, and support programs to integrate MVQ into the daily workflows of client-facing teams. Use feedback and metrics to continually improve the platform and value delivery.
  • Platform & Data Stewardship: Guide the ongoing development of the MVQ platform, working with Technology and Analytics partners to integrate new data sources, product value models, and analytical capabilities. Ensure the platform is robust, intuitive, and compliant with Mastercard’s standards.
  • People Leadership: Build and lead a high-performing MVQ team. Provide mentorship, coaching, and clear direction to develop talent and expertise. Foster a collaborative, innovative, and results-driven culture that embraces a value-first mindset and grows the next generation of value experts.


Key Skills & Qualifications

  • Extensive experience: 15+ years in strategy, program management, consulting, or related roles leading large-scale, cross-functional initiatives in complex organizations (payments, financial services, or B2B technology sector preferred).
  • Strategic & analytical acumen: Proven strength in strategic thinking, financial analysis, and data-driven problem solving. Adept at developing ROI models and translating analytics into actionable business insights.
  • Value & commercial expertise: Familiarity with value-based selling, pricing strategy, and customer value management. Ability to articulate and quantify the business value of technology or service solutions to customers and internal stakeholders.
  • Leadership & influence: Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and influence senior executives and diverse stakeholders. Skilled at driving alignment in a matrixed environment through vision and collaboration.
  • Change management: Demonstrated success in driving adoption of new tools, processes, or cultural initiatives at scale. Passion for evangelizing innovation, with a track record of leading organizational change and embedding new practices.
  • People development: Strong people management capabilities. Experience building and mentoring high-performing teams, developing talent, and fostering an inclusive, high-energy culture.


Leadership Attributes

  • Visionary & enterprise-minded: Strategic thinker who sees the big picture and can articulate a compelling, forward-looking vision for value quantification as a competitive differentiator.
  • Collaborative influencer: Excels at leading through influence and building consensus. Cultivates trust and teamwork across functions, geographies, and levels to achieve shared goals.
  • Results-focused & accountable: Driven to deliver tangible results. Sets high standards, measures progress and takes ownership to ensure execution and business impact.
  • Innovative & adaptable: Embraces change with a continuous improvement mindset. Stays ahead of industry trends (e.g., data analytics, AI) to evolve capabilities and encourage creative solutions.
  • Authentic & inclusive leader: Exemplifies Mastercard’s values and Decency Quotient (DQ). Creates a culture of integrity, inclusion, and empowerment. Inspires and develops others through mentorship and leading by example.


Success Measures

  • Widespread Adoption: MVQ is widely utilized across Mastercard’s businesses, with strong engagement from product, sales, account management, and customer success teams globally.
  • Measurable Impact: MVQ drives tangible commercial outcomes – influencing key deal wins, supporting value-based pricing and renewals, and helping demonstrate ROI to customers – resulting in improved client satisfaction, retention, and revenue growth.
  • Executive Alignment: MVQ is championed by senior leadership as a critical enterprise capability. Its insights are regularly incorporated into strategic planning, business reviews, and go-to-market decisions, reinforcing Mastercard’s value-driven culture and competitive differentiation.


Corporate Security Responsibility

 All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.
