descriptionHtml:
Nomura Overview:


Nomura is a global financial services group with an integrated network spanning approximately 30 countries and regions. By connecting markets East & West, Nomura services the needs of individuals, institutions, corporates and governments through its three business divisions: Wealth Management, Investment Management, and Wholesale (Global Markets and Investment Banking). Founded in 1925, the firm is built on a tradition of disciplined entrepreneurship, serving clients with creative solutions and considered thought leadership. For further information about Nomura, visit www.nomura.com.


Nomura Services, India supports the group’s global businesses. With world-class capabilities in trading support, research, information technology, financial control, operations, risk management and legal support, the firm plays a key role in facilitating the group’s global operations.


At Nomura, creating an inclusive workplace is a priority. Our approach to inclusion encompasses a variety of initiatives, including sensitization campaigns, implementing conducive policies & programs, providing infrastructure support and engaging in community events. Over time, we have made meaningful progress in these areas, and this commitment has been well-recognized across the industry. We are proud recipients of the prestigious Top 10 Employers award by the India Workplace Equality Index (IWEI), IWEI Gold Employer of Choice awards, India CSR Leadership Award 2024 for Holistic Village Development Program and the YUVA Unstoppable Changemaker Awards.


Divisional Overview:


Information Technology division supports the dynamic financial business through the development of strategic initiatives. IT partners with business to develop, implement and support sophisticated trading systems as well as middle and back-office systems through the user of state of art technology.


Business Overview:


International Wealth Management (IWM) as an Asian private bank, we have more than 50 years of experience delivering value to high-net-worth clients in Asia.



Job Role & Responsibilities:

  • Team lead for Avaloq development team in CE-FO domain.
  • Lead and guide the Avaloq development team in CE-FO domain through technical knowledge and functional expertise.
  • Lead and drive solution feasibility study/analysis, project effort sizing and ensure consistent and complete requirement and solution coverage.
  • Act as a functional SME at specific areas relating to CE-FO domain and able to go deep into requirement/solution gaps with support from product architects.
  • Lead and drive implementation for projects at Build/Test/Go-Live stages.
  • Translate, map and document business requirements from BRD to technical solution for delivery in FRS.
  • Able to develop and review code changes.
  • Act as key lead in supporting various testing phases of the project as well as production issues if arise.
  • Support projects go-live.
  • Participate/support in regional and/or global projects.


Job Requirements/Experience:

  • University bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science, computer engineering, information systems or banking.
  • At least 15 years of relevant working experience, preferably in the Technology space.
  • At least 12 years of work experience in Avaloq core banking platform and financial banking industry understanding particularly in Wealth Management domain.
  • Avaloq certified professional.
  • Good understanding of business product and process flows and product lifecycles.
  • Has implementation experience in medium to large scale projects.
  • Able to work independently with minimal supervision.
  • Able to work and organize with sense of urgency under multiple deadlines.
  • Able to multi-task and prioritize under stress.
  • Able to lead and guide team members under charge.
  • Adept in project management experience is preferred.
  • Well-verse in client output, cost and fee in Avaloq preferred.
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Willing to learn and able to adapt to different technologies.
  • Self-motivated, responsible and reliable team player.
  • Communicates well & across all levels.


We are committed to providing equal opportunities throughout employment including in the recruitment, training and development of employees. We prohibit discrimination in the workplace whether on grounds of gender, marital or domestic partnership status, pregnancy, career's responsibilities, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, race, color, national or ethnic origins, religious belief, disability or age


.

*Applying for this role does not amount to a job offer or create an obligation on Nomura to provide a job offer. The expression "Nomura" refers to Nomura Services India Private Limited together with its affiliates.

title: ESG Head
description: Overview As ESG Competency Head, you will have an opportunity to conduct customer engagements and shape & deliver the Sustainability Innovation program while working with Business Development Managers and other relevant stakeholders, globally. Your primary responsibility will be to identify, develop, and lead engagements with Enterprise customers – working with line of business and C-suite executives, enabling opportunities, facilitating sustainability engagements, and driving resultant prototypes & scaled solutions. You will work with managers, employees, customers, and other stakeholders to address the customers’ approach towards environmental responsibility with the goal to minimize adverse environmental, social or governance impacts and develop sustainability solutions. You will be working in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial, enterprise technology and sales environment with cross functional teams as many parts of the program will have interdependencies with other teams within the organization. Typically, cross-functional collaboration includes sales leadership, vertical-wise business development teams, solution architects, marketing, partners, professional services, and sales enablement. Key Responsibilities ▪ Oversee the overall execution of the organization’s Sustainability offerings program and enable customers to meet their sustainability targets & goals. ▪ Lead the formulation & development of sustainability strategies and policies, including setting standards for customers. ▪ Ensure customers follow industry best practices and are compliant with environmental, social & governance standards and legislations. ▪ Collaborate with appropriate stakeholders to facilitate sustainability performance evaluations that are timely and constructive. ▪ Actively network with potential clients and other professionals in the sustainability sector and create business opportunities.Essential Duties ▪ Develop business relationships with leading customer organizations and work with Sales leadership to identify & qualify customers for the Sustainability Innovation program. ▪ Lead and coordinate translation of sustainability solutions into working prototypes with relevant engineering and services teams as well as partners.▪ Build value propositions & develop business cases and effectively track & articulate the value of ongoing engagements including, major wins. ▪ Train & enable local sales teams to help qualify customers, and lead elements of digital innovation engagement. ▪ Work with the Alliance management teams to identify, engage, and enable partners who can accelerate an approach towards sustainability innovation. ▪ Conduct customer briefings & accelerate the organization’s ESG solution-led initiatives through participation in Sustainability events, Thought leadership articles etc. ▪ Execute the strategic business development plan while working with key internal stakeholders (e.g., sales, service teams, legal, support, etc.). ▪ Handle a high volume of engagements and the fast pace of the emerging Hi-Tech industry vertical. ▪ Draft and prepare reports, presentations, proposals, and recommendations to the Chief Sustainability Officer, C-Suite of client organizations, and other key stakeholders. ▪ Prepare documentation required for project funding for various customers or sustainability initiatives including proposals and grant applications. ▪ Develop and maintain appropriate documentation & records, including budgets and other financials. ▪ Consider sustainability proposals with attention to factors such as cost effectiveness, feasibility, and ease of integration with other programs. ▪ Evaluate efficacy of sustainability programs; recommend and implement improvements as necessary. ▪ Identify creative ways in which the customers can balance business obligations with the goal of respecting, supporting, and adhering to the local & global regulations.Skills & Qualities ▪ Leadership experience as well as strong verbal and written communications skills are a must. ▪ Domain & Functional expertise in corporate sustainability as well as Sustainability solutions. ▪ Experience in ESG business development, consulting, product management & sustainability innovation. (IT solution development is a plus) ▪ Superlative and creative problem-solving and analytical skills. ▪ Thorough working knowledge of sustainability practices, and applicable laws and regulations. ▪ Ability to analyze and understand research to identify relevant and valuable information. ▪ Extremely proficient with MS Office or related software. ▪ Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail. ▪ Ability to create, interpret, and execute a budget. ▪ Presentation skills to deliver proposals to varying groups of individuals. Qualifications ▪ A Bachelor’s degree from a recognized institution or university. ▪ A Master’s degree in Environment, Management or equivalent (Background in Quantitative field such as Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics etc. is a plus). ▪ 10+ years of relevant work experience in enterprise markets. Breadth and depth in multiple business verticals and industry ecosystems. ▪ Published thought leadership in sustainability transformation. ▪ Passion for sustainability – deep experience & understanding of enterprise operations (including, characteristics of large & global businesses) and delivery & implementation of successful sustainability projects.

title: Global Capability Center Director
description: At Zimmer Biomet, we believe in pushing the boundaries of innovation and driving our mission forward. As a global medical technology leader for nearly 100 years, a patient’s mobility is enhanced by a Zimmer Biomet product or technology every 8 seconds.As a Zimmer Biomet team member, you will share in our commitment to providing mobility and renewed life to people around the world. To support our talent team, we focus on development opportunities, robust employee resource groups (ERGs), a flexible working environment, location specific competitive total rewards, wellness incentives and a culture of recognition and performance awards. We are committed to creating an environment where every team member feels included, respected, empowered and recognised.What You Can ExpectThe Global Capability Center (GCC) Director is responsible for end-to-end site leadership, including facilities management and overall operational stewardship, while providing solid-line people leadership and governance for the India Global Capability Center (GCC). This role ensures the physical and operational environment enables safe, compliant and efficient delivery of shared services globally. descriptionHtml:
At Zimmer Biomet, we believe in pushing the boundaries of innovation and driving our mission forward. As a global medical technology leader for nearly 100 years, a patient’s mobility is enhanced by a Zimmer Biomet product or technology every 8 seconds.

As a Zimmer Biomet team member, you will share in our commitment to providing mobility and renewed life to people around the world. To support our talent team, we focus on development opportunities, robust employee resource groups (ERGs), a flexible working environment, location specific competitive total rewards, wellness incentives and a culture of recognition and performance awards. We are committed to creating an environment where every team member feels included, respected, empowered and recognised.

What You Can Expect

The Global Capability Center (GCC) Director is responsible for end-to-end site leadership, including facilities management and overall operational stewardship, while providing solid-line people leadership and governance for the India Global Capability Center (GCC). This role ensures the physical and operational environment enables safe, compliant and efficient delivery of shared services globally. In addition, this leader plays a critical role in establishing the GCC as a hub for technology, digital innovation capabilities, enabling global product development, data and platform teams to scale delivery through strong culture, talent and cross-functional ways of working within a highly matrixed and global operating model.

The Director owns talent management, performance outcomes, workforce planning, and organizational health for the India GCC and partners closely with global functional leaders who assist with work direction, task prioritization, skill development and functional expertise. Operating within a highly matrixed environment, this leader balances enterprise priorities, local execution, and cross-functional alignment to drive service excellence, engagement, and continuous improvement.

Work Location: Bangalore

Work Mode: Hybrid (3 Days in office)

How You'll Create Impact

  • Serve as the senior site leader, representing the organization externally and partnering with HR and business leaders to drive safety, engagement, and culture.
  • Provide solid-line leadership for the India GCC, including performance management, recruiting, talent reviews, development planning, and employment decisions.
  • Partner closely with global functional leaders who provide work direction and functional expertise, ensuring strong alignment within a matrixed operating model.
  • Oversee all aspects of facilities management, including maintenance, safety, security, space planning, and regulatory compliance.
  • Own site-level governance and accountability for service delivery performance, including SLAs, KPIs, risk management, and stakeholder satisfaction.
  • Lead budgeting and financial stewardship for facilities and site operations, driving cost optimization and value creation.
  • Oversee vendor relationships and outsourced service providers, ensuring performance, compliance, and integration with shared services.
  • Act as the primary escalation point for site, people, facilities, or delivery issues, leading cross-functional resolution.
  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives and support global transformation programs impacting site operations and service delivery.
  • Build and sustain a high-performance, inclusive culture across the GCC, with particular focus on enabling technology, digital, and innovation teams to collaborate effectively with global counterparts.
  • Partner with global functional leaders (e.g., IT, DTS, R&D, Quality, Clinical, Procurement) to enable consistent ways of working, clear decision rights, and effective governance across a matrixed delivery model.
  • Support the attraction, development, and retention of critical technical talent (e.g., software engineering, data, AI, digital platforms) by fostering strong onboarding, career pathways, engagement mechanisms, and leadership capability at the site.
  • Enable execution of global digital, technology, and innovation priorities by proactively identifying and removing site-level, organizational, or operational barriers to delivery

What Makes You Stand Out

  • Strong people leadership skills with experience managing managers and senior leaders.
  • Ability to lead effectively in a global, matrixed environment, balancing solid-line accountability with cross-functional partnership.
  • Proven stakeholder management and influencing skills across functions, geographies, and cultures.
  • Clear, confident communicator able to translate complex issues for senior audiences and drive alignment.
  • Track record of building engaged, high-performing teams and driving accountability and results.
  • Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to quickly diagnose issues, lead resolution, and communicate outcomes.
  • Working knowledge of continuous improvement methodologies (e.g., Lean, Kaizen, Six Sigma).
  • Sound judgment, executive presence, and resilience in a fast-paced, high-visibility role.
  • Ability to partner effectively with senior technology, digital, and product leaders, with sufficient technical fluency to support innovation-focused teams without acting as a functional expert.
  • Demonstrated capability in building organizational culture and engagement in environments with a high concentration of engineering, digital, or innovation roles.

Your Background

Required

  • Bachelor's Degree and 15+ years relevant experience
  • Experience leading a Shared Services Center, Global Capability Center, BPO, or similarly complex global operation of 200+ FTE.
  • Experience working within a matrixed global operating model.
  • Fluency in English required; additional languages preferred.

Preferred

  • Master’s degree
  • 10+ years of progressive leadership experience, including managing managers.
  • Continuous improvement experience or certification (e.g., Six Sigma).
  • Experience operating within regulated or quality-driven environments (e.g., ISO standards).
  • Experience supporting or scaling technology, digital, or product development organizations (e.g., software engineering, data & analytics, digital platforms) within a global or matrixed enterprise.
  • Exposure to or sponsorship of AI, advanced analytics, or digital innovation initiatives is strongly preferred.

Travel Expectations

Up to 25%

EOE/M/F/Vet/Disability

title: Director, GTM Operations

However, we do look for alignment, so make sure you can explain why your interest and experience are right for each specific role.Reapplying. If you’re applying for the same role again, please wait six months from your last decision before hitting submit.
descriptionHtml:
About Pleo

Messy spend management is tricky business. And tedious processes are a lose-lose situation for all involved, not just finance. At Pleo, we're changing that. We build spend solutions that make managing money seamless, empowering, and surprisingly effective for finance teams and employees alike - with a vision to help all businesses ‘go beyond’.

The word ‘Pleo’ actually means ‘more than you’d expect’, and living by that mantra has been the secret to our success over the last 10 years.

Now, we’re at a pivotal moment in our journey; every move we make has a direct impact on our 40,000+ customers, our business, and our collective success. We need people who take pride in uncovering customer needs, who turn complex problems into simple solutions, challenge the way things are done (respectfully), and always aim high. With great ambitions driving us forward, we can’t say we’ve got this whole thing figured out. And frankly, that’s half the fun! What we can say is that we’re a driven, progressive, and, importantly, a kind bunch of 850+ people from over 100 nationalities, all committed to delivering the future of business spending, together.

About The Role

We’re looking for a Director, GTM Operations to join our Revenue Operations function at Pleo. In this role, you’ll help architect and execute the engine that powers our global growth and be part of the RevOps leadership team as we scale our market presence across Europe. If you’re excited about building world- class operational frameworks from the ground up and are passionate about the intersection of data, technology and human performance in FinTech, then this is the opportunity for you!

Who You’ll Be Working With And Reporting To

You’ll report to our VP of Revenue Operations and work closely with teams in Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success. Our team of 12 direct reports is highly collaborative and dedicated to maximising revenue efficiency and GTM excellence. You’ll also have the chance to partner with teams across Strategy, Finance, Data and Product to ensure seamless execution of our commercial strategy.

What You’ll Be Doing

As a Director, GTM operations, you will:

  • Execute initiatives to improve the end-to-end customer journey, ensuring our GTM motion is frictionless and scalable
  • Collaborate with the Revenue Leadership team to develop innovative solutions for territory management, quota setting, and lead management
  • Analyse complex data sets to inform board-level-decision making and long-term commercial strategy
  • Provide framework support regional commercial teams and VP Commercial
  • Mentor a high-performing team of 12 to ensure professional growth and high-impact delivery across the RevOps function

What You Bring

You’ll thrive in this role if you have:

  • A solid understanding of B2B SaaS sales cycles, cadences, rituals and customer lifecycle metrics
  • Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate with C-suite stakeholders and VPs
  • A passion for solving complex organisational problems in creative and innovative ways
  • Excellence in time management and the ability to prioritise high-leverage tasks in a fast-paced environment
  • Experience with advanced HubSpot architecture and RevOps tooling
  • A background in managing a large operational team within a high-growth scale-up environment

Why is his role a good fit for for you

This role is a good fit for you if:

  • You enjoy the building & iteration phase and want to put your stamp on a global GTM strategy in an evolving business environment
  • You are a hands on leader who finds genuine joy in coaching and developing their team
  • You believe RevOps is a strategic partner to the business, not just a support function

This role is not a good fit for you if:

  • You prefer a steady state where all processes are already defined and documented
  • You prefer being an individual contributor over managing a large, diverse team
  • You find ambiguity frustrating rather than an opportunity to create order

How You’ll Develop In This Role

In your first few months at Pleo, you’ll:

  • Lead the GTM operating cycle with GTM leadership
  • Collaborate with sales & CX leadership to improve the velocity and win rates through better process optimisation and management
  • Grow your skills in leadership, taking on new challenges as the company continues its journey toward becoming the #1 spending solution in Europe.

Please note: We can hire on a remote, hybrid or in-person set-up in any of the locations listed on the advert but you will need to be physically based in the country of your choice with a valid right to work. We are unable to offer visa sponsorship for this role in any of the listed locations.

Show Me The Benefits

  • Your own Pleo card (no more out-of-pocket spending!)
  • Lunch is on us for your work days - enjoy catered meals or receive a lunch allowance based on your local office
  • Comprehensive private healthcare - depending on your location, coverage options include Vitality, Alan or Médis
  • We offer 25 days of holiday + your public holidays
  • For our Team, we offer both hybrid and fully remote working options
  • We use MyndUp to give our employees access to free mental health and well-being support with great success so far
  • Paid parental leave - we want to make sure that we're supportive of families and help you feel that you don't have to compromise your family due to work

The interview process

We want to ensure you are set-up for success and understand what will be expected of you. If your application is successful, our interview process is as follows:

  • Intro call: A 30-minute chat with our Talent Acquisition Partner to discuss the role and your background.
  • HM Interview stage: Up to 45 mins interview with the Hiring Manager to go deeper on your experience and how your profile can adapt to the role
  • Peer Interview: 30 minutes with potential future colleagues, it's designed to assess cultural add
  • Pleo Challenge: We will send you a take home test and invite you to present it during a 60 minute team interview. This is an opportunity to show us how you would handle the day to day of the role
  • Final Interview: A final interview with a Senior Leader

Transparency is important to us so we also wanted to share some insights about what we’re looking for in applications to ensure you can set yourself up for success!

Last time we hired for this role, we received a vast amount of applications but only a few were selected for an intro call. With this in mind, we want to help you understand what we really care about when reviewing your application:

  • We receive a lot of CVs, and many of them are AI-generated. We love seeing people leverage AI—it’s a big focus for us internally too—but without human intervention, these CVs can sometimes become generic and fail to show a candidate in the best light. What we're really looking for is the specific details of real impact that only you—not AI—know from your previous experience. A top tip from us is to use the “Achieved X, as measured by Y, by doing Z” formula (credit: Laszlo Bock, :2014) to give a really clear picture of what you’ve worked on. A final note: including links to your previous companies' websites is a huge help and allows us to truly understand your background.
  • Every single application we receive is reviewed by a human (yes, hundreds of them) because we believe that candidates' efforts should be matched by an equal level of human care. This means that we expect a similar level of attention put into your application. Read and answer the application questions carefully, they make a huge difference in our decision-making process.
  • Highlight most important aspects of the role, points that might get misunderstood, any specific industry requirements etc.

About Your Application

  • English first. Since it's our company language, please submit your application in English. You’ll be using it a lot if you join us.
  • A fair look for everyone. Our talent team reads every single application to ensure the process is fair. To keep things running smoothly, we only accept applications through our system—our support team can’t pass on calls or emails.
  • Diversity drives us. We can only reach our goals if our team reflects the world around us. That starts with you hitting apply, even if you don't tick every single box. We encourage people from all backgrounds and experiences to join us.
  • Interview at your best. We want you to feel comfortable throughout the process. If you have any accessibility requirements or need a specific format, email belonging@pleo.io. We’ll design a process that works for you.
  • Your data is safe. When you apply, we process your personal data as a data processor. For more information on how Pleo processes personal data, read our Privacy Policy here.
  • Applying for multiple roles? Nothing is stopping you, and we assess every role independently. However, we do look for alignment, so make sure you can explain why your interest and experience are right for each specific role.
  • Reapplying. If you’re applying for the same role again, please wait six months from your last decision before hitting submit.

title: Director of Engineering
workType: Engineering, Health Care Provider, and Product Management
sector: IT Services and IT Consulting, Software Development, and Hospitals and Health Care
description: Job Summary: We are seeking a highly experienced and technical Engineering Director to lead our engineering teams and manage multiple teams within the Source engineering organization in India. This individual will be responsible for establishing a high-tech engineering culture, attracting and hiring top talent, providing full support to a business value stream, and developing team members. The ideal candidate will have extensive experience in building distributed cloud systems with high availability and scale and will be accountable for the entire product delivery. Key Responsibilities: Leadership and Management: Lead and manage engineering teams, fostering a culture of accountability and high performance. Establish and maintain a high-tech engineering culture, promoting innovation and continuous improvement. Attract, hire, and retain top engineering talent, reducing attrition and developing team members. Provide technical leadership and mentorship to engineering teams, ensuring alignment with company goals and objectives. Lead and support cross-functional teams within a specific value stream by fostering close partnerships with business stakeholders. Champion the alignment of technical and domain-specific solutions with strategic objectives, ensuring timely delivery and a steadfast commitment to quality. Serve as a key liaison, driving collaboration, accountability, and continuous improvement across the organization. Provide strategic leadership and mentorship by guiding team members in both technical and domain areas. Leverage coaching opportunities to foster professional growth, enhance individual capabilities, and elevate the overall skillset and performance of the team. Product Delivery: Oversee the entire product development lifecycle, from concept to delivery, ensuring high-quality and timely releases. Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including product management, to align objectives, priorities, and trade-offs. Drive the execution and delivery of features by coordinating with multiple teams and stakeholders. Drive high-quality, on-time product delivery by leveraging industry best practices and AI-driven solutions to meet client needs. Operate with a metrics-focused mindset to transparently report progress, identify trends, and uncover opportunities for continuous improvement and innovation. Technical Expertise: Provide technical guidance and direction for the development of distributed cloud systems with high availability and scale. Stay updated with the latest technological advancements and introduce new technologies to improve products and drive innovation. Ensure the scalability, reliability, and performance of applications, systems, and integrations. Actively contribute to the internal Architecture Review Board by evaluating proposed solutions and offering expert recommendations that promote scalability, long-term maintainability, and architectural integrity of the Source platform. Champion the integration of AI throughout the Software Development Lifecycle, advocating for its strategic adoption to accelerate delivery and elevate product quality. Operational Excellence: Develop and implement metrics to measure performance, efficiency, and scalability of products and services. Drive performance optimization initiatives using data analytics and key performance indicators (KPIs). Establish and maintain a comprehensive quality management system, ensuring the highest levels of quality in deliverables.  Qualifications: Bachelor's or Master's degree in computer science, Engineering, or a related field. 15+ years of experience in software engineering, including 5+ years in leadership roles, with a strong track record of delivering enterprise-scale solutions and driving high-performing teams. Strong technical background with expertise in .Net, C#, Java, databases, AWS/Azure cloud, SaaS, Angular and/or React, and design and architectural patterns. Experience in managing and leading globally distributed engineering teams in a highly agile/scrum environment. Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Proven ability to attract, hire, and retain top engineering talent. Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to make critical decisions that contribute to the success of the product.  Preferred Qualifications: Experience in Healthcare Technology and highly regulated industries. Experience in building and managing distributed cloud systems with high availability and scale. Demonstrated experience or certification in prompt engineering, particularly in the context of AI-assisted development.
descriptionHtml:

title: Director, Quality Engineering & Regulatory Operations
description: About VelseraMedicine moves too slow. At Velsera, we are changing that.Velsera was formed in 2023 through the shared vision of Seven Bridges and Pierian, with a mission to accelerate the discovery, development, and delivery of life-changing insights.Velsera provides software and professional services for:AI-powered multimodal data harmonization and analytics for drug discovery and developmentIVD development, validation, and regulatory approvalClinical NGS interpretation, reporting, and adoptionWith our headquarters in Boston, MA, we are growing and expanding our teams located in different countries!The Director, Technical Quality & Regulatory Operations is accountable for the operational execution of Velsera's Quality Management System (QMS) and regulatory quality requirements within an engineering-led organization. Reporting to the CTO, this role ensures that regulatory and quality requirements (FDA QMSR, ISO 13485, IVDR/MDR) are translated into effective, inspection-ready processes embedded into product development and operations. In the absence of a standalone Regulatory Affairs organization, this role owns regulatory operations and inspection readiness, while regulatory strategy, submissions, and external commitments remain with executive leadership. Information Security and GRC are intentionally separate to preserve independence and audit credibility.What will you do?Own and continuously improve the ISO 13485-aligned Quality Management System (QMS), including document control, training, management review, CAPA, non-conformances, and quality metrics. Ensure inspection readiness and host FDA inspections, IVDR/MDR Notified Body audits, and customer quality audits. Operationalize regulatory requirements into engineering and operational processes, including design controls, risk management facilitation (ISO 14971), and IEC 62304-aligned SDLC governance. Maintain inspection-ready Design History Files (DHF), traceability, release, and change control processes. Establish and maintain a supplier quality and validation oversight program for critical vendors and service providers. Apply 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11 requirements pragmatically where applicable (regulated records, audit trails, e-signatures, validated workflows). Lead and scale a high-performing Quality Operations team. Partner with Information Security & GRC leadership under an Integrated Management System (IMS) governance model, sharing audit artifacts while preserving separation of duties. Explicitly Out of Scope:Regulatory strategy, submission planning, and formal agency correspondence ownership. Security control ownership, SOC 2 attestation, or ISO 27001 certification ownership. RequirementsWhat do you bring to the table?10+ years of experience in Quality / Regulatory Operations within regulated healthcare, SaMD, IVD, or life sciences software. Deep expertise in ISO 13485, FDA QMSR (21 CFR 820), IVDR/MDR, ISO 14971, and IEC 62304. Demonstrated ownership of audits, CAPA programs, and inspection readiness. Strong ability to operate effectively within an engineering-led organization. Preferred: Experience operating within an Integrated Management System (IMS). Working knowledge of enterprise security, privacy, and GxP expectations to support cross-functional audits. Experience scaling compliance in AI-driven or clinical genomics platforms. BenefitsFlexible Work & Time Off - Embrace hybrid work models and enjoy the freedom of unlimited paid time off to support work-life balanceHealth & Well-being - Access comprehensive group medical and life insurance coverage, along with a 24/7 Employee Assistance Program (EAP) for mental health and wellness supportGrowth & Learning - Fuel your professional journey with continuous learning and development programs designed to help you upskill and growEngaging & Fun Work Culture - Experience a vibrant workplace with team events, celebrations, and engaging activities that make every workday enjoyable& Many More..
descriptionHtml:
About Velsera

Medicine moves too slow. At Velsera, we are changing that.

Velsera was formed in 2023 through the shared vision of Seven Bridges and Pierian, with a mission to accelerate the discovery, development, and delivery of life-changing insights.

Velsera provides software and professional services for:

  • AI-powered multimodal data harmonization and analytics for drug discovery and development
  • IVD development, validation, and regulatory approval
  • Clinical NGS interpretation, reporting, and adoption

With our headquarters in Boston, MA, we are growing and expanding our teams located in different countries!

The Director, Technical Quality & Regulatory Operations is accountable for the operational execution of Velsera's Quality Management System (QMS) and regulatory quality requirements within an engineering-led organization. Reporting to the CTO, this role ensures that regulatory and quality requirements (FDA QMSR, ISO 13485, IVDR/MDR) are translated into effective, inspection-ready processes embedded into product development and operations. In the absence of a standalone Regulatory Affairs organization, this role owns regulatory operations and inspection readiness, while regulatory strategy, submissions, and external commitments remain with executive leadership. Information Security and GRC are intentionally separate to preserve independence and audit credibility.

What will you do?

  • Own and continuously improve the ISO 13485-aligned Quality Management System (QMS), including document control, training, management review, CAPA, non-conformances, and quality metrics.
  • Ensure inspection readiness and host FDA inspections, IVDR/MDR Notified Body audits, and customer quality audits.
  • Operationalize regulatory requirements into engineering and operational processes, including design controls, risk management facilitation (ISO 14971), and IEC 62304-aligned SDLC governance.
  • Maintain inspection-ready Design History Files (DHF), traceability, release, and change control processes.
  • Establish and maintain a supplier quality and validation oversight program for critical vendors and service providers.
  • Apply 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11 requirements pragmatically where applicable (regulated records, audit trails, e-signatures, validated workflows).
  • Lead and scale a high-performing Quality Operations team.
  • Partner with Information Security & GRC leadership under an Integrated Management System (IMS) governance model, sharing audit artifacts while preserving separation of duties.

Explicitly Out of Scope:

  • Regulatory strategy, submission planning, and formal agency correspondence ownership.
  • Security control ownership, SOC 2 attestation, or ISO 27001 certification ownership.

Requirements

What do you bring to the table?

  • 10+ years of experience in Quality / Regulatory Operations within regulated healthcare, SaMD, IVD, or life sciences software.
  • Deep expertise in ISO 13485, FDA QMSR (21 CFR 820), IVDR/MDR, ISO 14971, and IEC 62304.
  • Demonstrated ownership of audits, CAPA programs, and inspection readiness.
  • Strong ability to operate effectively within an engineering-led organization.

Preferred:

  • Experience operating within an Integrated Management System (IMS).
  • Working knowledge of enterprise security, privacy, and GxP expectations to support cross-functional audits.
  • Experience scaling compliance in AI-driven or clinical genomics platforms.

Benefits

  • Flexible Work & Time Off - Embrace hybrid work models and enjoy the freedom of unlimited paid time off to support work-life balance
  • Health & Well-being - Access comprehensive group medical and life insurance coverage, along with a 24/7 Employee Assistance Program (EAP) for mental health and wellness support
  • Growth & Learning - Fuel your professional journey with continuous learning and development programs designed to help you upskill and grow
  • Engaging & Fun Work Culture - Experience a vibrant workplace with team events, celebrations, and engaging activities that make every workday enjoyable
  • & Many More..

title: Head of Hunter Platform and Products - DA
description: Company DescriptionExperian unlocks the power of data to create opportunities for consumers, businesses and society. We gather, analyze and process data in ways others can’t. We help individuals take financial control and access financial services, businesses make smarter decision and thrive, lenders lend more responsibly, and organizations prevent identity fraud and crime. For more than 125 years, we’ve helped consumers and clients prosper, and economies and communities flourish – and we’re not done. Our 17,800 people in 45 countries believe the possibilities for you, and our world, are growing. We’re investing in new technologies, talented people and innovation so we can help create a better tomorrow.Job DescriptionExperian India is seeking a strategic and visionary senior leader to head its Fraud and ID Verification Solutions team. This client-facing role is central to driving solution excellence, market leadership, and product innovation in the financial services sector. The ideal candidate will be a recognized expert in fraud prevention and identity verification, capable of translating client challenges into impactful solutions while influencing product strategy and market execution.In today’s era of data-driven decision-making, Experian is uniquely positioned to solve client problems by being at the forefront of data analysis, solutioning, and innovation. Our platform-first approach enables seamless integration of data from multiple sources, creating a one-stop solution for the financial industry in India.Key ResponsibilitiesClient Engagement & Strategic SolutioningLead a team of specialists to engage with clients, deeply understand their fraud and ID verification challenges, and strategically position Experian’s solutions to address these needs.Collaborate with Sales teams on day-to-day sales activities, providing strategic guidance and solution expertise to support deal progression and closure.Ensure that analytics-driven insights are embedded into client conversations and product evolution.Ensure that proposed solutions are optimal, scalable, and aligned with client needs and regulatory frameworks.Post-Sales Success & AdoptionOversee the transition from sales to onboarding, ensuring smooth implementation and client satisfaction.Drive client adoption and usage of solutions, acting as a strategic advisor to maximize descriptionHtml:
Company Description

Experian unlocks the power of data to create opportunities for consumers, businesses and society. We gather, analyze and process data in ways others can’t. We help individuals take financial control and access financial services, businesses make smarter decision and thrive, lenders lend more responsibly, and organizations prevent identity fraud and crime. For more than 125 years, we’ve helped consumers and clients prosper, and economies and communities flourish – and we’re not done. Our 17,800 people in 45 countries believe the possibilities for you, and our world, are growing. We’re investing in new technologies, talented people and innovation so we can help create a better tomorrow.

Job Description

Experian India is seeking a strategic and visionary senior leader to head its Fraud and ID Verification Solutions team. This client-facing role is central to driving solution excellence, market leadership, and product innovation in the financial services sector. The ideal candidate will be a recognized expert in fraud prevention and identity verification, capable of translating client challenges into impactful solutions while influencing product strategy and market execution.

In today’s era of data-driven decision-making, Experian is uniquely positioned to solve client problems by being at the forefront of data analysis, solutioning, and innovation. Our platform-first approach enables seamless integration of data from multiple sources, creating a one-stop solution for the financial industry in India.

Key Responsibilities

Client Engagement & Strategic Solutioning

  • Lead a team of specialists to engage with clients, deeply understand their fraud and ID verification challenges, and strategically position Experian’s solutions to address these needs.
  • Collaborate with Sales teams on day-to-day sales activities, providing strategic guidance and solution expertise to support deal progression and closure.
  • Ensure that analytics-driven insights are embedded into client conversations and product evolution.
  • Ensure that proposed solutions are optimal, scalable, and aligned with client needs and regulatory frameworks.

Post-Sales Success & Adoption

  • Oversee the transition from sales to onboarding, ensuring smooth implementation and client satisfaction.
  • Drive client adoption and usage of solutions, acting as a strategic advisor to maximize value realization.

Product Feedback & Innovation

  • Serve as the voice of the customer to internal product teams, providing actionable insights on product performance and enhancement opportunities.
  • Influence the development of new features and capabilities based on client feedback and market trends.
  • Work closely with Experian’s highly proficient analytics teams to co-create innovative, data-led solutions.
  • Identify trends across liabilities and assets onboarding, spanning both consumer and MSME segments.
  • Leverage advanced analytics to address fraud not only from a consumer perspective but also through digital device footprints, behavioral signals, and emerging fraud typologies.

Go-To-Market Strategy

  • Partner with GTM and sales teams to launch new Fraud and ID Verification products.
  • Define target segments, positioning, messaging, and market entry strategies.
  • Support enablement and training of sales teams on new offerings.

Industry Leadership & Advocacy

  • Represent Experian at fraud forums, conferences, and industry events.
  • Establish Experian as the leading authority on fraud and ID verification in India’s financial services sector.
  • Build and maintain relationships with key stakeholders across banks, NBFCs, and regulatory bodies.

Community Building & Thought Leadership

  • Lead the planning and execution of the Annual Fraud Closed User Group Summit for Hunter platform users and industry leaders.
  • Manage the Hunter Advisory Board, a quarterly forum of top financial institutions to discuss product evolution and regulatory developments.

Cross-Functional Leadership

  • Collaborate across product, customer success, onboarding, analytics, and GTM teams to drive holistic client outcomes.
  • Inspire and lead a high-performing team of specialists, fostering a culture of excellence, accountability, and innovation.

Qualifications

Qualifications & Experience:

  • 15+ years of experience in fraud prevention, identity verification, or related domains within financial services or technology.
  • Proven experience in strategic client engagement and solutioning.
  • Strong understanding of the fraud landscape in India’s financial services sector.
  • Experience in product strategy, GTM planning, and cross-functional leadership.
  • Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and strategic thinking skills.
  • Prior experience in hosting industry summits or advisory boards is a plus.

What Success Looks Like

  • Strategic influence on sales outcomes and client satisfaction.
  • High adoption and usage rates post-implementation.
  • Strong market presence and thought leadership in the fraud domain.
  • Continuous product improvement driven by client insights.
  • Seamless collaboration across internal teams and external stakeholders.
  • Delivery of innovative, data-driven solutions that address emerging fraud patterns.

Why us

  • We’re a high-performance and driven team but we don’t forget to celebrate success
  • We offer strong career and international opportunities for high performers
  • We invest heavily in our products and our people
  • We offer training and support from experienced subject matter experts and managers plus dedicated learning & development time

Additional Information

  • We are looking for someone who:
  • Is a consultative business professional
  • Is confident, clear, certain and consistent
  • Is able to work alongside a sales team managing multiple opportunities concurrently
  • Passionate, resourceful, gracious and adaptable
  • High moral standards, honesty and an ethical approach to doing business
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving skills and ability to see the big-picture & develop conceptual solutions
  • Candidates that have prior relevant work experience, excel in ambiguous situations, and possess a passion for helping companies become more competitive
  • Business acumen combined with a comfort with technology is a must

Experian Careers - Creating a better tomorrow together

Find out what its like to work for Experian by clicking here

descriptionHtml:
Role Overview:

  • Position: Senior Director of Software Engineering
  • Reporting to: Senior Vice President of Technology


Primary Responsibility: Oversee the architecture, design, and development of scalable technology solutions, while mentoring and managing a team of technical leads, full-stack engineers, and architects.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Lead the architecture, design, and development of scalable technology solutions.
  • Drive best engineering and operational practices.
  • Lead and mentor a team of technical leads, full-stack engineers, and architects.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams (product, analytics, business units) to ensure technology solutions meet business needs.
  • Provide strategic direction and foster a culture of innovation and continuous improvement.
  • Ensure data integrity, security, scalability, efficiency, and availability across platforms.
  • Stay current with emerging technologies (AI/ML) and integrate them into the product.
  • Communicate technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders effectively.

Qualifications:

  • 15+ years of experience in engineering, with at least 4+ years in a management role.
  • Strong leadership and management skills, especially in globally distributed teams.
  • Experience with Agile methodologies and a passion for high-quality software delivery.
  • Excellent communication skills and the ability to manage complex projects independently.

Desired Attributes:

  • Strong customer focus, multitasking ability, and a desire to learn and implement best practices.
  • Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, customer-oriented environment.
  • Proven ability to lead and mentor technology teams, including full-stack engineers, technical leads, and architects.


About us:

Belzabar Software Design is a world-class software boutique with headquarters in the US and an impressive development record that spans a number of major projects. Every day, millions of people use the software developed by Belzabar.

Belzabar Software develops Web and mobile-based products for diverse clientele that includes Fortune 500 companies, government agencies, and Web startup ventures. We excel at developing advanced systems that solve an organisation challenge optimally, from performance and mathematical optimisation perspectives.

What we do: We work on prominent, high-performance projects for prominent organisations as well as innovative startups

title: Head of Machine Learning, India
description: About EarnInAs one of the first pioneers of earned wage access, our passion at EarnIn is building products that deliver real-time financial flexibility for those with the unique needs of living paycheck to paycheck. Our community members access their earnings as they earn them, with options to spend, save, and grow their money without mandatory fees, interest rates, or credit checks.We’re fortunate to have an incredibly experienced leadership team, combined with world-class funding partners like A16Z, Matrix Partners, DST, Ribbit Capital, and a very healthy core business with a tremendous runway. We’re growing fast and are excited to continue bringing world-class talent onboard to help shape the next chapter of our growth journey. POSITION SUMMARY EarnIn is a fintech company where machine learning is embedded across our platform capabilities to power business decisions and customer experiences at scale. From risk and fraud to growth, personalization, and operational efficiency, ML systems are mission‑critical to our success. Ensuring the reliability, performance, and scalability of these systems in production is therefore a core engineering priority. The Head of Machine Learning, India, will be responsible for the success of ML systems built and operated by our India teams. This role sits at the intersection of applied science and engineering excellence, with a strong emphasis on translating research and experimentation into robust, high‑performance production systems. The ideal candidate has deep hands‑on experience deploying ML at scale, thrives in fast‑growing environments, and brings both theoretical grounding and strong production engineering instincts.As EarnIn continues to expand in India, this role will inherit a high‑performing machine learning team, with clear potential to grow and scale the organization over time. As the senior ML leader in‑country, the role may also include site leadership responsibilities, partnering closely with HR and Talent Acquisition on hiring, culture building, and team engagement. This position will be hybrid from our Bengaluru office, as part of our expanding site, with two+ days a week in the office. EarnIn provides excellent employee benefits, including healthcare, internet/cell phone reimbursement, a learning and development stipend, and opportunities to collaborate with and travel to our Mountain View HQ and Bangkok Site. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. WHAT YOU'LL DO Build, manage, and mentor a high‑performing team of machine learning engineers; foster collaboration, support career growth, and invest in continuous skills development.Own the delivery and impact of ML initiatives, working closely with cross‑functional partners to ensure measurable business outcomes.Lead the design, development, and optimization of ML‑based systems, including techniques such as deep learning and reinforcement learning.Drive a culture of scientific and engineering rigor balanced with real‑world pragmatism, with a strong focus on experimentation, evaluation, and quantifying impact on key engagement and business metrics.Partner with data engineering, product management, and marketing teams to take ML solutions from conception through experimentation to reliable, timely production deployment.Establish India as a strategic ML hub for EarnIn by leading hiring efforts, onboarding, and close partnership with HR and TA on culture building, team health, and local engagement initiatives.WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR MS or Ph.D. in Computer Science, Statistics, or a closely related technical field.7+ years of industry experience, including 3+ years of people management experience.Strong depth across core machine learning algorithms and applied ML techniques.Proven experience building, deploying, and operating production ML systems at scale.Advanced proficiency in Python and SQL, with a strong software engineering foundation.Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate across technical and non‑technical stakeholders.  At EarnIn, we believe that the best way to build a financial system that works for everyday people is by hiring a team that represents our diverse community. Our team is diverse not only in background and experience but also in perspective. We celebrate our diversity and strive to create a culture of belonging. EarnIn does not unlawfully discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), gender identity, gender expression, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, age, physical or mental disability, legally protected medical condition, family care status, military or veteran status, marital status, registered domestic partner status, sexual orientation, genetic information, or any other basis protected by local, state, or federal laws. EarnIn is an E-Verify participant. EarnIn does not accept unsolicited resumes from individual recruiters or third-party recruiting agencies in response to job postings. No fee will be paid to third parties who submit unsolicited candidates directly to our hiring managers or HR team.
descriptionHtml:
About EarnIn

As one of the first pioneers of earned wage access, our passion at EarnIn is building products that deliver real-time financial flexibility for those with the unique needs of living paycheck to paycheck. Our community members access their earnings as they earn them, with options to spend, save, and grow their money without mandatory fees, interest rates, or credit checks.

We’re fortunate to have an incredibly experienced leadership team, combined with world-class funding partners like A16Z, Matrix Partners, DST, Ribbit Capital, and a very healthy core business with a tremendous runway. We’re growing fast and are excited to continue bringing world-class talent onboard to help shape the next chapter of our growth journey.

POSITION SUMMARY 
EarnIn is a fintech company where machine learning is embedded across our platform capabilities to power business decisions and customer experiences at scale. From risk and fraud to growth, personalization, and operational efficiency, ML systems are mission‑critical to our success. Ensuring the reliability, performance, and scalability of these systems in production is therefore a core engineering priority. The Head of Machine Learning, India, will be responsible for the success of ML systems built and operated by our India teams. This role sits at the intersection of applied science and engineering excellence, with a strong emphasis on translating research and experimentation into robust, high‑performance production systems. The ideal candidate has deep hands‑on experience deploying ML at scale, thrives in fast‑growing environments, and brings both theoretical grounding and strong production engineering instincts.
As EarnIn continues to expand in India, this role will inherit a high‑performing machine learning team, with clear potential to grow and scale the organization over time. As the senior ML leader in‑country, the role may also include site leadership responsibilities, partnering closely with HR and Talent Acquisition on hiring, culture building, and team engagement. This position will be hybrid from our Bengaluru office, as part of our expanding site, with two+ days a week in the office. EarnIn provides excellent employee benefits, including healthcare, internet/cell phone reimbursement, a learning and development stipend, and opportunities to collaborate with and travel to our Mountain View HQ and Bangkok Site. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. WHAT YOU'LL DO 
  • Build, manage, and mentor a high‑performing team of machine learning engineers; foster collaboration, support career growth, and invest in continuous skills development.
  • Own the delivery and impact of ML initiatives, working closely with cross‑functional partners to ensure measurable business outcomes.
  • Lead the design, development, and optimization of ML‑based systems, including techniques such as deep learning and reinforcement learning.
  • Drive a culture of scientific and engineering rigor balanced with real‑world pragmatism, with a strong focus on experimentation, evaluation, and quantifying impact on key engagement and business metrics.
  • Partner with data engineering, product management, and marketing teams to take ML solutions from conception through experimentation to reliable, timely production deployment.
  • Establish India as a strategic ML hub for EarnIn by leading hiring efforts, onboarding, and close partnership with HR and TA on culture building, team health, and local engagement initiatives.
WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR 
  • MS or Ph.D. in Computer Science, Statistics, or a closely related technical field.
  • 7+ years of industry experience, including 3+ years of people management experience.
  • Strong depth across core machine learning algorithms and applied ML techniques.
  • Proven experience building, deploying, and operating production ML systems at scale.
  • Advanced proficiency in Python and SQL, with a strong software engineering foundation.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate across technical and non‑technical stakeholders.

 

At EarnIn, we believe that the best way to build a financial system that works for everyday people is by hiring a team that represents our diverse community. Our team is diverse not only in background and experience but also in perspective. We celebrate our diversity and strive to create a culture of belonging. EarnIn does not unlawfully discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), gender identity, gender expression, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, age, physical or mental disability, legally protected medical condition, family care status, military or veteran status, marital status, registered domestic partner status, sexual orientation, genetic information, or any other basis protected by local, state, or federal laws. EarnIn is an E-Verify participant. 

EarnIn does not accept unsolicited resumes from individual recruiters or third-party recruiting agencies in response to job postings. No fee will be paid to third parties who submit unsolicited candidates directly to our hiring managers or HR team.

title: Senior Director, FinOps
sector: Software Development, Computer and Network Security, and Information Technology & Services
description: Flexera saves customers billions of dollars in wasted technology spend. A pioneer in Hybrid ITAM and FinOps, Flexera provides award-winning, data-oriented SaaS solutions for technology value optimization (TVO), enabling IT, finance, procurement and cloud teams to gain deep insights into cost optimization, compliance and risks for each business service. Flexera One solutions are built on a set of definitive customer, supplier and industry data, powered by our Technology Intelligence Platform, that enables organizations to visualize their Enterprise Technology Blueprint™ in hybrid environments—from on-premises to SaaS to containers to cloud.We’re transforming the software industry.  We’re Flexera.  With more than 50,000 customers across the world, we’re achieving that goal. But we know we can’t do any of that without our team. Ready to help us re-imagine the industry during a time of substantial growth and ambitious plans?  Come and see why we’re consistently recognized by Gartner, Forrester and IDC as a category leader in the marketplace. Learn more at flexera.comAs Senior Director, FinOps , you will own the product strategy, portfolio leadership, and execution for Flexera’s rapidly expanding FinOps and Cloud Financial Management (CFM) solutions. You will define and communicate a compelling longterm vision for how enterprises and service providers optimize cloud, SaaS, and softwaredriven technology spend , and lead teams in executing against that vision at scale.This role is accountable for ensuring Flexera continues to deliver the most comprehensive FinOps platform in the market , spanning multicloud cost visibility, optimisation, commitment management, automation, and emerging areas such as FinOps for AI and data platforms. You will work crossfunctionally with engineering, product marketing, sales, finance to ensure strong business outcomes, customer value, and market leadership.Key Accountabilities & ResponsibilitiesMarket & Customer LeadershipDevelop a deep understanding of Flexera’s FinOps business, customers, and buyer personas (FinOps, Finance, Engineering, Procurement, MSPs), as well as evolving market trends and the competitive landscape. Clearly articulate Flexera’s differentiated value across the FinOps lifecycle (inform, optimize, operate).Portfolio Vision & StrategySet and continuously refine the longterm vision and strategy for the FinOps product portfolio, balancing innovation, platform cohesion, and commercial outcomes to maintain category leadership.People & Organizational LeadershipDefine clear team and individuallevel roles, expectations, and success measures. Hire, develop, and retain top product talent, fostering strong performance, accountability, and career progression across senior product leaders and managers.Roadmap Ownership & Investment DecisionsOwn the endtoend product roadmap, including build / buy / partner decisions, ensuring alignment with Flexera’s broader platform strategy and business objectives.Engineering Partnership & Delivery ExcellencePartner with engineering leadership to continuously improve the SDLC, emphasizing agile product discovery, iterative delivery, automation, and scalable cloudnative architectures.CrossFunctional InfluenceLead and influence across Flexera’s business units with a collaborative, customercentric mindset, aligning product, sales, marketing, finance, and services around shared outcomes.Customer & Ecosystem EngagementEngage directly with customers, prospects, analysts, and partners to inform product strategy, validate roadmap priorities, and support strategic growth opportunities.Outcome Measurement & Value RealizationDefine and track success metrics across financial performance, delivery milestones, customer adoption, and realized savings/value, ensuring strong ROI for both customers and Flexera.Flexera is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.  Qualified applicants will be considered for open roles regardless of age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local/national laws, policies and/or regulations. Flexera understands the value that results from employing a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workforce. We recognize that equity necessitates acknowledging past exclusion and that inclusion requires intentional effort. Our DEI (Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion) council is the driving force behind our commitment to championing policies and practices that foster a welcoming environment for all.We encourage candidates requiring accommodations to please let us know by emailing careers@flexera.com.
descriptionHtml:
Flexera saves customers billions of dollars in wasted technology spend. A pioneer in Hybrid ITAM and FinOps, Flexera provides award-winning, data-oriented SaaS solutions for technology value optimization (TVO), enabling IT, finance, procurement and cloud teams to gain deep insights into cost optimization, compliance and risks for each business service. Flexera One solutions are built on a set of definitive customer, supplier and industry data, powered by our Technology Intelligence Platform, that enables organizations to visualize their Enterprise Technology Blueprint™ in hybrid environments—from on-premises to SaaS to containers to cloud.

We’re transforming the software industry.  We’re Flexera.  With more than 50,000 customers across the world, we’re achieving that goal. But we know we can’t do any of that without our team. Ready to help us re-imagine the industry during a time of substantial growth and ambitious plans?  Come and see why we’re consistently recognized by Gartner, Forrester and IDC as a category leader in the marketplace. Learn more at flexera.com

As Senior Director, FinOps , you will own the product strategy, portfolio leadership, and execution for Flexera’s rapidly expanding FinOps and Cloud Financial Management (CFM) solutions. You will define and communicate a compelling longterm vision for how enterprises and service providers optimize cloud, SaaS, and softwaredriven technology spend , and lead teams in executing against that vision at scale.

This role is accountable for ensuring Flexera continues to deliver the most comprehensive FinOps platform in the market , spanning multicloud cost visibility, optimisation, commitment management, automation, and emerging areas such as FinOps for AI and data platforms. You will work crossfunctionally with engineering, product marketing, sales, finance to ensure strong business outcomes, customer value, and market leadership.

Key Accountabilities & Responsibilities

Market & Customer Leadership

Develop a deep understanding of Flexera’s FinOps business, customers, and buyer personas (FinOps, Finance, Engineering, Procurement, MSPs), as well as evolving market trends and the competitive landscape. Clearly articulate Flexera’s differentiated value across the FinOps lifecycle (inform, optimize, operate).

Portfolio Vision & Strategy

Set and continuously refine the longterm vision and strategy for the FinOps product portfolio, balancing innovation, platform cohesion, and commercial outcomes to maintain category leadership.

People & Organizational Leadership

Define clear team and individuallevel roles, expectations, and success measures. Hire, develop, and retain top product talent, fostering strong performance, accountability, and career progression across senior product leaders and managers.

Roadmap Ownership & Investment Decisions

Own the endtoend product roadmap, including build / buy / partner decisions, ensuring alignment with Flexera’s broader platform strategy and business objectives.

Engineering Partnership & Delivery Excellence

Partner with engineering leadership to continuously improve the SDLC, emphasizing agile product discovery, iterative delivery, automation, and scalable cloudnative architectures.

CrossFunctional Influence

Lead and influence across Flexera’s business units with a collaborative, customercentric mindset, aligning product, sales, marketing, finance, and services around shared outcomes.

Customer & Ecosystem Engagement

Engage directly with customers, prospects, analysts, and partners to inform product strategy, validate roadmap priorities, and support strategic growth opportunities.

Outcome Measurement & Value Realization

Define and track success metrics across financial performance, delivery milestones, customer adoption, and realized savings/value, ensuring strong ROI for both customers and Flexera.

Flexera is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.  Qualified applicants will be considered for open roles regardless of age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local/national laws, policies and/or regulations. 

Flexera understands the value that results from employing a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workforce. We recognize that equity necessitates acknowledging past exclusion and that inclusion requires intentional effort. Our DEI (Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion) council is the driving force behind our commitment to championing policies and practices that foster a welcoming environment for all.

We encourage candidates requiring accommodations to please let us know by emailing careers@flexera.com.

title: Vice President, Data Quality Lead Engineer
description: About This RoleBlackRock is seeking a Data Quality Framework Lead to lead the strategy, architecture, and delivery of a core capability within Enterprise Data Platform in Aladdin Data. This role combines platform engineering, data governance, and stakeholder leadership to build a scalable, trusted, and transparent framework for data quality across the firm.The platform ensures that the data BlackRock relies on is fit for purpose across key dimensions including completeness, accuracy, timeliness, consistency, validity, and integrity. It provides clear and actionable quality signals to upstream producers, downstream systems, and end users so data can be used confidently for decisions at scale.The framework uses custom Python operators, Great Expectations, and Airflow-orchestrated pipelines to perform quality checks as data moves through the ecosystem. The ideal candidate brings strong technical depth, sound architectural judgment, hands-on execution, and the ability to align stakeholders around a common platform vision.What You Will DoLead the evolution of BlackRock’s data quality framework as a strategic platform capability for validating and monitoring data across the Aladdin Data ecosystem.Define the technical direction for a metadata-driven framework that supports reusable quality rules, policy enforcement, exception handling, quality scoring, and domain-level service standards.Design and deliver controls that run within Airflow-orchestrated pipelines, enabling early detection of issues before they affect downstream systems or clients.Build a strong operating model for observability, transparency, and remediation so producers and consumers can identify and resolve issues quickly.Partner with engineering, product, governance, and business stakeholders to drive adoption, prioritization, and long-term roadmap execution.Key ResponsibilitiesOwn the target-state architecture for the Data Quality Framework, including rule execution patterns, validation layers, quality gates, exception workflows, and extensibility standards.Build and scale platform services, libraries, and APIs for rule authoring, execution, scoring, auditability, and quality SLA and SLO reporting across datasets and domains.Develop controls across core quality dimensions, including completeness, accuracy, timeliness, consistency, validity, uniqueness, and referential integrity.Design and implement profiling, anomaly detection, and drift detection capabilities covering schema changes, null patterns, distribution shifts, outliers, volume trends, and freshness checks.Implement reconciliation and financial control patterns such as source-to-target checks, row-count balancing, aggregate validation, hashes, and critical total checks.Drive adoption of Great Expectations and custom Python operators to standardize how assertions are defined, executed, versioned, and reused across pipelines.Integrate the framework into Airflow-based data pipelines so checks run at the right control points with meaningful alerting and triage.Establish metadata-driven rule management, including ownership, lineage, versioning, parameterization, execution history, and audit-ready evidence.Optimize framework performance across high-volume environments, particularly Snowflake and MSSQL, balancing control rigor with runtime efficiency.Create clear visibility for downstream platforms, internal users, and clients through dashboards, scorecards, status indicators, and actionable exception reporting.Mentor engineers and act as a senior technical leader who can make pragmatic architecture decisions while staying hands-on when needed.Influence enterprise standards for trusted data consumption in partnership with data governance, platform engineering, and product teams.Required QualificationsAt least 10+ years of experience in backend, data platform, or data engineering roles, with a strong record of hands-on technical delivery.Deep expertise in Python and experience building reusable engineering frameworks, services, or platform capabilities.Strong experience with workflow orchestration and pipeline integration, ideally with Airflow in complex enterprise environments.Proven experience designing and implementing data quality controls across batch and or near-real-time data pipelines.Strong understanding of enterprise data quality operating models, including SLAs and SLOs, exception handling, issue triage, and remediation workflows.Hands-on experience with Great Expectations or similar data quality frameworks, with the ability to extend them through custom engineering patterns.Strong proficiency with Snowflake and or MSSQL, including query tuning, scalable control execution, and performance optimization.Experience with metadata-driven platform design, data modeling, lineage, traceability, and audit-ready control frameworks.Demonstrated ability to make architecture decisions, influence platform direction, and communicate effectively with senior technical and business stakeholders.Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience.Preferred QualificationsExperience building
